scenes.title: A Craft and a Grove
scenes.intro: A stretch of Runehold's land falls into dispute: the Brown want it for a stone-quarry, the Green for a sacred grove. Both have sent gifts ahead of their asking, and both will remember which way you rule.

advisors.text: The quarry means good stone and a guild in our debt, Archon. A grove means birdsong. Rule with your head.
A grove is a living thing that gives for a hundred years; a quarry is a hole. The Green will not forget which you chose.

choices.label: Grant the land to the Brown for a quarry

win.text: The Brown break ground with a will, and good stone and Brown goodwill flow to Runehold — while the Green mourn a grove unplanted.
win.saga: You gave the disputed land to the Brown for a quarry, gaining stone and the delta's favour and the Green's cold shoulder.

choices.label: Grant the land to the Green for a grove

win.text: The Green plant a grove that bears within the year, and their friendship blooms with it — while the Brown pack up their tools, unpaid and unpleased.
win.saga: You gave the disputed land to the Green for a grove, gaining harvest and the jungle's favour and the Brown's resentment.

choices.label: Divide the land between them

win.text: You draw a line both can live with, and your fairness wins a grudging nod from each. A quarry and a grove, and no enemy made.
win.saga: You split the disputed land fairly between Brown and Green, and your even hand won a grudging warmth from both.

lose.text: Your compromise pleases no one — each side sure the other got the better half, and both a little colder for it.
lose.saga: Your attempt to split the disputed land satisfied neither Brown nor Green, cooling both against Runehold.

scenes.title: A Dormant Ember Found
scenes.intro: A dying wanderer spends his last strength on a gift: the location of a dead hold far to the east, and the thing still banked in its cold hearth. "Not out — sleeping," he rasps. "An Ember, dormant, waiting for hands to carry it somewhere it can burn again." It is a long road through hard country to fetch it home, and country like that is full of others who would want a sleeping fire for themselves.

advisors.text: Every Ember we bring home is a candle against the long dark, Archon. If one sleeps out there waiting for hands, let them be our hands. Send a party to bear it home.
Send them, but send them careful. A sleeping Ember on an open road is a prize half the ruin would kill for. Whoever goes must be as good at not being seen as at fighting.

choices.label: Send a party to bear the Ember home

win.text: You choose two hardy enough for the long east road and send them to fetch a sleeping fire out of a dead man's map. If they carry it home, Runehold is one shard nearer whole.
win.saga: You sent a party east to recover a dormant Ember from a dead hold and bear it home to Runehold.

choices.label: Let the sleeping Ember lie

win.text: You mark the dead man's map and set it aside. The east road is long and hungry, and a fire that has slept this long can sleep a while longer — until Runehold can spare the hands to fetch it safely.
win.saga: You let a rumoured dormant Ember lie in its dead hold rather than spend a party on the long east road.

scenes.title: A Map in the Ash
scenes.intro: A pedlar trades you a scrap of skin-vellum for a loaf and a lie: a map, old past reckoning, marking a nameless pyramid out in the ash-flats. "Older than the Burning," he says, "and never opened. What's inside remembers things the world's let go." It would take a party of your own the better part of a year to reach it and return.

advisors.text: A sealed place older than the Burning, Archon, in a world that is forgetting? If there is memory anywhere, it is there. Worth the Wizards it would cost us for a season.
It would strip the Circle of two while they are gone — no casting, no counsel from them. Weigh that before you send anyone into a tomb on a pedlar's word.

choices.label: Send a party to the nameless pyramid

win.text: You mark the road and choose who goes. Two of the Circle shoulder packs and set out into the ash; it will be seasons before you know what they found.
win.saga: You sent a party of the Circle into the ash-flats to open the nameless pyramid marked on a pedlar's old map.

choices.label: Keep the Circle whole — burn the map

win.text: You feed the map to the hearth-rune. Whatever the pyramid remembers, it can keep — Runehold cannot spare the hands.
win.saga: You burned the pedlar's map to the nameless pyramid rather than spare the Wizards to open it.

scenes.title: A Rumour of Embers
scenes.intro: A half-frozen wanderer is carried in from the passes, and before the hearth-rune he weeps — he knows this fire. "Yours is not the only one," he rasps. "When the Tower fell we broke the Flame and ran, each with a shard, to hearths no enemy could find. I have seen another still burning — north and east, in a hold whose name I dare not say aloud."

advisors.text: If he speaks true, Archon, then Runehold is not alone — and a Flame gathered back to itself is a Flame that might one day be tended whole again.
Or it is bait. Every fire out there has enemies, and following a stranger's map into the north is how covens end.

choices.label: Mark the road — the other Embers must be found

win.text: You set the wanderer's words to memory and to map. It is only a rumour, but the coven has a purpose now beyond survival: somewhere the Flame still burns in pieces, and the pieces can be sought.
win.saga: A half-frozen wanderer swore another Ember still burns north and east, and you set his rumour to map — the shards of the Flame can be sought.

choices.label: Tend your own fire; the north can keep its ghosts

win.text: You give the wanderer broth and a corner by the hearth, and let his rumour cool. Runehold has trouble enough of its own.
win.saga: A wanderer brought word of another burning Ember in the north, and you let the rumour cool, tending your own fire instead.

scenes.title: A Strayed Herd
scenes.intro: A herd of Elysian ponies — manes like banked coals, eyes too knowing — drifts down from the high passes and lingers in Runehold's lower meadow. They are no one's, for now.

advisors.text: Meat and hides through Deepfrost, Archon. The meadow will not feed us; they might.
Ride them! Tame one and you have a friend out of the Elysian Fields. Eat one and you have a sad winter dinner.

choices.label: Take the herd for meat and hide

win.text: The meadow runs red and the granary fills. The coven eats; something old in the high passes takes note.
win.saga: A masterless herd of Elysian ponies strayed into the meadow, and you took them for meat and hide — the granary filled, and something in the high passes took note.

choices.label: Try to gentle and keep the herd

win.text: Patient hands and quiet runes win them over. Runehold has mounts now, and a little wonder it had forgotten.
win.saga: A herd of Elysian ponies strayed down from the passes, and you gentled them to Runehold — mounts now, and a little forgotten wonder.

lose.text: The herd will not be held. By morning they are gone over the ridge, and you are left with trampled fields.
lose.saga: You tried to keep a strayed herd of Elysian ponies, but they broke away over the ridge by morning and left your fields trampled.

scenes.title: A Wandering Wizard
scenes.intro: A Wizard comes to Runehold's gate out of the ash — road-worn, wary, and looking for a hearth that still burns. "I can work," they say, showing a palm scarred with old runes. "I ask only a place by the fire."

advisors.text: Another pair of hands and another voice at the Circle, Archon. We are too few as it is.
Or another mouth to feed, and a stranger who knows where our Ember sleeps. Weigh them before you open the door.

choices.label: Welcome them into the Circle

win.text: You give the wanderer a place by the fire, and in the giving Runehold grows by one. Let us hope they were worth the bread.
win.saga: A rune-scarred Wizard came out of the ash asking for a hearth, and you welcomed them into the Circle — Runehold grew by one.

choices.label: Test their craft before you decide

win.text: You set them a hard working, and they meet it. A true Wizard — and now one of yours.
win.saga: A wandering Wizard sought a place at the fire; you set them a hard working, they met it, and you took them into the Circle.

lose.text: The working slips their grasp; whatever they were, they are not what Runehold needs. You send them on with bread and no place at the fire.
lose.saga: A wandering Wizard sought a place at the fire, but the working slipped their grasp, and you sent them on with bread and no place.

choices.label: Turn them away — the fire is small enough

win.text: You send the wanderer back to the road. The coven stays as it is: few, and known.
win.saga: A wandering Wizard asked for a place at the fire, and you turned them back to the road — the Circle stayed few, and known.

scenes.title: A Warrior at the Gate
scenes.intro: A Warrior war-band makes camp at the edge of Runehold's fields. Their captain — sword on her back, a hawk on her glove — sends word: she will guard your borders against the things the Fracture spits out. For a price, or for a pact.

advisors.text: Steel we cannot cast, but steel does not forget how to kill. Make the pact. Pride is cheap when the Imps come.
They cannot touch the Flame, and so they fear what we are. Bind ourselves to them and the coven will mutter.

choices.label: Swear a pact of mutual defense

win.text: You clasp the captain's forearm. Warriors walk your walls that night, and for the first time in a season the watch sleeps.
win.saga: A Warrior captain camped at your fields, and you swore a pact of mutual defence — Warriors walked your walls, and the watch slept at last.

choices.label: Pay them in coin, owe them nothing

win.text: Coin changes hands; the war-band patrols a season and asks no loyalty. A clean bargain, while it lasts.
win.saga: A Warrior war-band offered to guard your borders, and you paid them in coin, owing nothing — a clean bargain while it lasted.

choices.label: Turn them away — Wizards keep their own walls

win.text: You thank the captain and decline. The coven approves; the dark beyond the fields does not.
win.saga: A Warrior captain offered her sword for your borders, and you declined — the coven approved; the dark beyond the fields did not.

scenes.title: Draw from the Ember
scenes.intro: The coven's mana runs low, and the hearth-rune burns bright and close. The Ember itself is power — you could draw on it directly. But to reach into the Sacred Flame for your own workings is to draw its Regard, and the Flame remembers who takes from it.

advisors.text: The Ember is not a well to be dipped, Archon. Draw on it and it will look back at us — and you know what its looking costs.
It is power, and we are starving for it. A keeper who will not use the fire is only a caretaker of ashes.

choices.label: Draw power straight from the hearth-rune

win.text: You reach into the Ember and it gives — a flood of raw mana into every waiting rune. It gives, and it notices.
win.saga: You drew mana straight from the Ember; it filled the coven's reserves, and the Flame's Regard turned toward you.

choices.label: Take only a careful ember's worth

win.text: You take a bare, careful measure — enough to matter, little enough that the Flame barely stirs.
win.saga: You drew a careful measure of mana from the Ember, and the Flame barely stirred at it.

choices.label: Leave the Ember be

win.text: You take your hand from the fire. The coven will make do — and the Flame's regard is not one hair the closer.
win.saga: You refused to draw on the Ember for mere workings, and the coven took heart from your restraint.

scenes.title: The Envoy from the Gilded Reach
scenes.intro: A Red Wizard in counting-house silks arrives from the Gilded Reach — smiling, with a ledger under one arm and an offer on his lips.

advisors.text: The Red cult never smiles for free. But coin buys grain, and grain is no metaphor.

choices.label: Pay tribute to secure Red favour

win.text: The ledger closes with a soft clap. You are, for now, a friend of the Stock Sanctuary.
win.saga: A Red envoy of the Gilded Reach came with a ledger and an offer, and you paid tribute — a friend of the Stock Sanctuary, for now.

choices.label: Refuse — Runehold is no one's debtor

win.text: The envoy's smile thins as he goes. Word will travel.
win.saga: A Red envoy of the Gilded Reach pressed an offer on you, and you refused to become his debtor — his smile thinned, and word travelled.

scenes.title: Overtures to the Red
scenes.intro: A Red factor you once snubbed sends a careful letter. The Stock Sanctuary, it seems, would rather profit from Runehold than feud with it — if you will make the first gesture.

advisors.text: Enemies cost more than tribute. Swallow the pride, Archon. Red gold spends the same as any.

choices.label: Send a generous tribute to mend the breach

win.text: The gift is received with a counting-house smile. The feud cools to mere commerce.
win.saga: The Gilded Reach sought to mend an old feud, and you sent a generous tribute — the quarrel cooled to mere commerce.

choices.label: Offer Red factors trade rights in Runehold

win.text: Red stalls rise in your market square. Coin trickles in, and so does Red influence you cannot quite see.
win.saga: To mend the breach with Red, you granted its factors trade rights in Runehold — coin trickled in, and Red influence with it.

choices.label: Let the feud stand

win.text: You burn the letter. The coven likes a leader who holds a grudge — until the price of one comes due.
win.saga: Red offered to mend your old feud, and you burned the letter and let it stand — a grudge the coven admired, until its price came due.

scenes.title: Red's Mana-Crystals
scenes.intro: A factor of the Gilded Reach comes to market with a locked case of mana-crystals — charged, tradeable, and priced to the last coin. There are certified stones with a Red seal, and cheaper unsealed ones for a buyer willing to trust his own eye.

advisors.text: Coin for power, Archon — the oldest trade there is. The sealed stones are true. The cheap ones are cheap for a reason.
I can tell a charged crystal from a dead one at ten paces. Let me pick through the unsealed lot — we could rob him blind.

choices.label: Buy certified crystals from the Reach

win.text: The seal is good and the stones are full. Coin changes hands, and Runehold's reserves with it — a clean and costly bargain.
win.saga: You bought Red's certified mana-crystals for a heavy purse, and filled the coven's reserves cleanly.

choices.label: Buy the cheap unsealed lot on your own eye

win.text: Your read is true — the unsealed lot is fuller than the factor knew, and you carry off a bargain that stings his ledger.
win.saga: You judged Red's unsealed mana-crystals by your own lore and carried off a bargain of real charge.

lose.text: Half the lot is dead glass. You paid little, but you got little, and the factor's smile follows you out of the market.
lose.saga: You gambled on Red's cheap mana-crystals and got half a case of dead glass for your coin.

choices.label: Buy nothing — the coven is no one's customer

win.text: You wave the case shut. Runehold will gather its own power, in its own time.
win.saga: You turned away Red's mana-crystals, unwilling to buy what the coven could gather itself.

scenes.title: Steel at the Gate
scenes.intro: It came due at last. A war-band of the Warrior-Guild stands in Runehold's frost at first light — the same steel you drew on, years ago, come to collect. "You struck first, Wizard," their leader says, plain as weather. "We have long memories, and short patience left."

advisors.text: We knew this day would come the moment you chose it, Archon. Muster the coven — better to answer steel standing than kneeling.
Or buy them off, if there is anything left to buy them with. A hold that fights the Warriors to the last is a hold that ends.

choices.label: Meet them with rune and fire

win.text: Wards flare, the frost catches light, and the war-band breaks against Runehold's stones. Steel will think twice before it comes here again.
win.saga: The Warrior-Guild came to collect the blood-debt of the day you struck first, and you met them with rune and fire — the war-band broke on Runehold's stones.

lose.text: Your fire is not enough. They come over the walls, and Runehold pays in blood and grain for the day you drew first.
lose.saga: The Warrior-Guild came for the old blood-debt, and your fire was not enough — they came over the walls, and Runehold paid in blood and grain.

choices.label: Buy the old blood-debt off

win.text: It galls, and it costs, but coin closes the ledger. The war-band rides, and the grudge with them.
win.saga: The Warrior-Guild came to collect the old blood-debt, and you bought it off with coin — the war-band rode, and the grudge with them.

scenes.title: Storm of Wild Magic
scenes.intro: A storm rolls in that is not weather — a churning front of wild, uncolored magic, crackling off the hilltops. It could be harnessed for a great draught of mana, if the coven has the chaos-craft to ride it. If not, it will ride them.

advisors.text: This is my country, Archon. Wild magic answers those who do not flinch — give me leave and I will wring the storm dry.
Or we ward the hold and let it pass. A storm like that has scattered stronger covens than ours across the hillside.

choices.label: Harness the storm

win.text: The coven rides the wild front and bleeds it white — a tremendous draught of mana, and the storm spends itself harmlessly over the hills.
win.saga: The coven harnessed a storm of wild magic and drew a great draught of mana as it broke over Runehold.

lose.text: The storm will not be ridden. It lashes the hold as it passes — some mana clawed back, but the wards buckle and the Fracture gapes wider for it.
lose.saga: A storm of wild magic threw off the coven's grasp and lashed Runehold, widening the Fracture as it passed.

choices.label: Ward the hold and let it pass

win.text: You raise the wards and wait it out. A little residual mana settles on the stones, and the hold is whole.
win.saga: You warded Runehold against a wild-magic storm and let it pass, gathering only what settled after.

scenes.title: The Apprentices' Overreach
scenes.intro: You find the apprentices attempting a mana-gathering rite well above their station — circle chalked, hands shaking, eyes bright with ambition. They could pull it off and fill the reserves. They could also pull the roof down on themselves.

advisors.text: Let them reach, Archon. A coven that never lets its young overreach never learns anything worth knowing. Watch, and be ready.
That rite has hurt steadier hands than theirs. Stop them, drill them, and let them try it in five years when they will not die of it.

choices.label: Let them try, and watch closely

win.text: It holds. The rite comes off clean, the reserves fill, and the apprentices walk a foot taller for a month.
win.saga: You let the apprentices attempt a rite above their station; it held, filling the reserves and their pride together.

lose.text: The circle collapses in a backlash of raw magic. An apprentice is badly hurt, mana is spent for nothing, and the coven's nerve is shaken.
lose.saga: The apprentices' overreach collapsed in backlash; one was badly hurt, and the coven's mana and nerve went with it.

choices.label: Stop them and drill the basics

win.text: You break the circle and set them to fundamentals until their hands are steady. A little mana from the exercise, and a lesson well kept.
win.saga: You stopped the apprentices' overreach and drilled them in fundamentals, gathering a little mana and a lasting lesson.

scenes.title: The Athenaeum's Price
scenes.intro: A grey-robed reader of the Forgotten Athenaeum comes north with a locked book and a proposal: Blue will share what Runehold has forgotten — for a tithe of coin, and a debt of deference.

advisors.text: I trained under their lamps. The Athenaeum's word is good and their books are better. Pay them.
Deference to Blue today is deference to Blue forever. Memory we can earn ourselves — slower, but ours.

choices.label: Pay the tithe and take the book

win.text: The reader unlocks the book and lets your scribes copy through the night. Runehold remembers a little more of itself.
win.saga: A Blue reader of the Forgotten Athenaeum offered a locked book of what Runehold had forgotten, and you paid the tithe — your scribes copied it through the night.

choices.label: Offer a working of your own in exchange

win.text: You trade craft for craft. The reader leaves impressed, and Runehold keeps its coin and its pride.
win.saga: A Blue reader offered a locked book for coin and deference; you traded a working of your own instead, and kept your coin and your pride.

lose.text: Your working falters under the reader's polite gaze. The book goes home unopened, and word of the fumble travels.
lose.saga: You tried to trade a working for the Athenaeum's locked book, but your craft faltered under the reader's gaze, and the book went home unopened.

choices.label: Send the reader away — Runehold bows to no library

win.text: The reader bows, unbowed, and goes. The coven stands a little prouder over an emptier shelf.
win.saga: A Blue reader of the Athenaeum offered forgotten lore for a debt of deference, and you sent him away unbowed — prouder over an emptier shelf.

scenes.title: The Bleeding Seam
scenes.intro: A hairline seam of the Fracture has opened in the hillside, and it bleeds raw magic into the air like heat off a stone. There is mana here for the taking — but the Fracture is a wound, and working it invites it to open wider.

advisors.text: Behind heavy wards, we can drink from it and give little back, Archon. Careless, and we trade a full reserve for a torn hillside.
Or we seal it and lose the mana. A wound fed is a wound that grows — I would not feed this one.

choices.label: Tap the seam behind heavy wards

win.text: The wards hold, the seam gives up a rich draught of mana, and the Fracture barely widens for it. Cleanly done.
win.saga: The coven tapped a bleeding Fracture-seam behind heavy wards, drawing rich mana while the Fracture barely stirred.

lose.text: You get the mana — the wards do not hold. The seam yawns wider as you draw, and the hillside groans with the Fracture's growing.
lose.saga: The coven drew mana from a Fracture-seam, but the wards failed and the wound gaped wider for it.

choices.label: Seal the seam instead

win.text: You close the seam with patient wardwork. A little bled-off mana settles in your reserves, and the hillside stills.
win.saga: You sealed a bleeding Fracture-seam rather than work it, gathering only what had bled off and easing the wound.

scenes.title: The Brown Guild's Commission
scenes.intro: Master craft-wizards of the Brown delta send word: for a commission, they will come and reinforce Runehold's walls and works with earth-magic that outlasts the men who lay it. Practical magic, practically priced.

advisors.text: The Brown build things that do not fall down, Archon. Coin spent on their craft is coin you spend once. And they remember a good client.

choices.label: Commission the Brown to reinforce the hold

win.text: Brown crews work a season and leave Runehold's stones half again as strong, and the guild half again as warm toward you.
win.saga: You commissioned the Brown guild to reinforce Runehold; the hold stands stronger, and the delta counts you a client.

choices.label: Trade an apprentice's season of labour for the craft

win.text: You send an apprentice to learn at the delta for a season in lieu of coin. They come home a passable earth-crafter, and the Brown pleased.
win.saga: You traded an apprentice's season of delta labour for the Brown's craft, warming the guild and learning its earth-work.

choices.label: Decline the commission

win.text: You send the guild's word back unanswered. Runehold's walls will stand as they are — and the Brown will remember being turned down.
win.saga: You declined the Brown guild's commission, cooling the delta's regard for Runehold.

scenes.title: The Chronomancer's Bargain
scenes.intro: A Yellow chronomancer rides in from the Riviera, robes stitched with turning glass, and offers to read the threads of Runehold's coming seasons — for a price, and a warmer word between your hearth and hers.

advisors.text: Time-sight is rare and rarely offered, Archon. What she sees could spare us a disaster — and the Yellow are worth having as friends.

choices.label: Accept the chronomancer's reading

win.text: She reads the year's threads over a long night and leaves you the wiser and the Yellow the fonder.
win.saga: You paid a Yellow chronomancer to read Runehold's coming seasons, warming the coven's standing with the Riviera.

choices.label: Haggle for the reading

win.text: You match her lore for lore until she laughs and reads for half her price — impressed, and a friend for it.
win.saga: You out-argued a Yellow chronomancer for a cheaper reading, and won her respect and the Riviera's regard.

lose.text: She runs rings around your bargaining and names her full price anyway. You pay it, a little smaller than you began.
lose.saga: A Yellow chronomancer out-haggled you; you paid her full price for the reading, warming Yellow but the poorer for it.

choices.label: Send the chronomancer on her way

win.text: You thank her and decline. She shrugs — time will tell, she says — and rides for the Riviera a shade cooler toward you.
win.saga: You turned away a Yellow chronomancer's reading, cooling Runehold's standing with the Riviera.

scenes.title: The Cold Hearth
scenes.intro: The hold the wanderer named is a ruin, and its hearth is grey ash gone hard as stone. You are too late by a lifetime: this Ember guttered out long ago, and the little hold that kept it went out with it. Only char-marks on the walls remember there was ever a fire here — that, and a single scorched book the cold spared.

advisors.text: Read the book before the wind takes it. A hearth that died has more to teach us than one that lived — this is how our own fire ends, if we let it.
The Athenaeum would give a great deal for what's on these pages. And so, Archon, should we.

choices.label: Take the scorched book and study the dead hearth

win.text: The pages tell of the slow going-out: workings that took twice the mana, then ten times, then none at all — until the last keeper could not remember the word for fire. A grim gift, but a gift. You understand the Forgetting a little better now, and how near it always is.
win.saga: You reached a hold the wanderer named and found its Ember long dead; from its one scorched book you learned how the Forgetting takes a hearth, word by word.

lose.text: The book crumbles to flakes before you can read it deeply. You carry away only the cold, and the knowledge that another Ember is gone for good.
lose.saga: You reached a dead Ember's ruined hearth, but its last book crumbled before you could read it — you carried away only the cold.

choices.label: Wake an ember from the cold ash with your own Flame

win.text: You touch a shaving of your own hearth-rune to the dead ash — and for one impossible breath, it glows. It does not last. But you have proven the Flame remembers itself across its shards, and that is worth more than the mana it cost.
win.saga: At a dead hold's cold hearth you touched your own Flame to the ash, and for one breath it glowed — proof the Flame remembers itself across its shards.

lose.text: The cold ash drinks your Flame and gives nothing back. Worse — something in the dead hearth stirs at the offering, and does not settle when you leave.
lose.saga: At a dead hold's cold hearth your Flame woke nothing — but something in the dead ash stirred at the offering, and did not settle.

scenes.title: The Coven Divided
scenes.intro: The Circle quarrels openly now. Two camps have formed at the hearth, and both look to you to name which Runehold will be: a coven of careful memory, or a coven that dares the Flame.

advisors.text: Tell them we are keepers of memory first. Caution has kept us alive this long.
Tell them we are children of the Flame. A coven that fears its own god deserves no apprentices.

choices.label: Declare for memory and caution

win.text: You name Runehold a house of remembrance. The careful are heartened; the bold go quiet, and a little cold.
win.saga: With the Circle split between caution and daring, you named Runehold a house of memory — the careful were heartened, the bold went cold.

choices.label: Declare for the Flame and daring

win.text: You name Runehold a house of the Flame. The devout blaze up; the cautious bite their tongues and watch.
win.saga: With the Circle split, you named Runehold a house of the Flame — the devout blazed up, the cautious bit their tongues and watched.

choices.label: Refuse to choose — hold the Circle together

win.text: You speak for an hour and somehow both camps leave believing they won. It will not hold forever. It holds tonight.
win.saga: With the Circle split between memory and Flame, you refused to choose and held both camps together — for tonight, at least.

lose.text: Your careful words satisfy no one. Both camps decide you are weak, and say so.
lose.saga: You tried to hold the divided Circle together without choosing, and satisfied no one — both camps judged you weak, and said so.

scenes.title: The Deepfrost Alignment
scenes.intro: The old charts come true: at Deepfrost's dark the far lights align as they do once in a long age, and for one night the world's magic runs close to the surface. A coven that knows to draw at the peak can gather more than a season's worth.

advisors.text: The alignment holds for one night only, Archon. Draw at the peak. A gift like this does not come again in most keepers' lives.

choices.label: Draw deep at the alignment's peak

win.text: The coven gathers through the long dark and the reserves run over — a season's mana and more, poured out of one aligned night.
win.saga: You drew deep at the Deepfrost alignment, and one aligned night gave Runehold more than a season's mana.

choices.label: Draw, and share the rite with a neighbour cult

win.text: You gather well, and send word of the peak to the Blue Bastion so their scholars might draw too. Less for your reserves — more for a friendship.
win.saga: You drew at the Deepfrost alignment and shared the rite with Blue, trading a fuller reserve for a warmer friendship.

scenes.title: The Disputed Relic
scenes.intro: Your diggers unearth a pre-Burning reliquary — a coil of rune-etched copper that hums when touched. Word travels fast. Within a week both Red and Blue send envoys, each certain the relic is theirs by right.

advisors.text: Red will pay in coin we can spend this winter. Sentiment does not fill a granary.
Sell it to a counting-house and it vanishes into a vault. Give it to the Athenaeum and it is studied, copied, remembered.

choices.label: Sell the relic to the Gilded Reach

win.text: Red coin floods in; the copper coil disappears into a Stock Sanctuary vault, and Blue does not forget the slight.
win.saga: Your diggers unearthed a humming pre-Burning reliquary, and you sold it to the Gilded Reach — Red coin flooded in, and Blue did not forget the slight.

choices.label: Entrust the relic to the Athenaeum

win.text: Blue scholars weep over the coil and share their findings freely. Red marks the lost sale in a ledger somewhere.
win.saga: Your diggers unearthed a humming pre-Burning reliquary, and you entrusted it to the Athenaeum — Blue shared its findings freely; Red marked the lost sale.

choices.label: Keep the relic and study it yourselves

win.text: Your own scribes coax the coil's secret loose. Both cults leave empty-handed and faintly insulted.
win.saga: Your diggers unearthed a humming pre-Burning reliquary, and you kept it to study — your scribes coaxed its secret loose, and both cults left insulted.

lose.text: The coil stays silent for you, and now both cults know you hoarded it for nothing.
lose.saga: You kept the unearthed reliquary to study yourselves, but it stayed silent for you — and both cults learned you had hoarded it for nothing.

scenes.title: The Drowned Conjuror
scenes.intro: Apprentices sorting the salvage bring you a water-swollen tome, giggling and uneasy. It is the account of Maelor, who in the last bright age tried to raise a drowned city whole from the seabed — towers, gardens, ten thousand souls — in a single working. The final pages are written in a shaking hand: he could summon the towers, then only the gardens, then only a single drowned bell, and at the last could not recall the word for 'up'.

advisors.text: He did not fail because he was weak, Archon. He failed because the memory was going out from under him mid-working. Read it as a warning: the further we reach, the further we have to fall.
A city from the sea! Give me the mana and I'd finish what Maelor started. He simply ran out of Flame — we haven't yet.

choices.label: Set the apprentices to copy and study the tome

win.text: You have the tome copied twice against loss, and its lesson read aloud in the hall: great workings die not from too little power but from too little memory. The coven is wiser, and a little more afraid.
win.saga: You had Maelor's drowned-city tome copied and its lesson read aloud — that great workings die not from too little power but too little memory.

choices.label: Shelve it — the coven needs hope, not omens

win.text: You lock Maelor's drowned city away where the apprentices won't brood on it. Some mornings a keeper needs to believe the reaching still works.
win.saga: You locked away Maelor's tome of the drowned city he could not raise, sparing the apprentices its omen — some mornings a keeper must believe the reaching still works.

scenes.title: The Fading Rune
scenes.intro: A master-rune carved into the hall's keystone has begun to blur. Left alone, its working will be gone by the next thaw.

advisors.text: Memory is the only wealth the cataclysm cannot burn. Pour mana into the archive while we still can.

choices.label: Pour mana into the archive

win.text: Apprentices chant through the night; the rune deepens and holds.
win.saga: A master-rune in the hall's keystone had begun to blur, and you poured mana into it through the night until it deepened and held.

choices.label: Let it fade — we have more pressing needs

win.text: By morning the keystone is smooth and silent, and no one alive recalls what it said.
win.saga: You let the blurring master-rune in the hall's keystone fade — by morning the stone was smooth, and no one alive recalled what it said.

scenes.title: The Flickering Door
scenes.intro: A door breathes light at the foot of the stair. Behind it, the Sacred Flame waits — and the coven is forgetting faster than it can learn.

advisors.text: We forget faster than we learn. The Flame remembers everything. Use it.
And what walks back out wearing our faces? I have buried Souls before, Archon.

choices.label: Send an apprentice into the Flame

win.text: She returns wreathed in old knowledge, eyes bright and strange. The lost runes are lost no longer.
win.saga: With the coven forgetting faster than it could learn, you sent an apprentice into the Sacred Flame — she returned wreathed in old knowledge, the lost runes with her.

lose.text: What returns is not her. The Circle does not speak for three days.
lose.saga: You sent an apprentice into the Sacred Flame for the lost runes, and what returned was not her — the Circle did not speak for three days.

choices.label: Seal the door. Some things should stay forgotten.

win.text: You wall the stair with rune and stone. The light goes out beneath your hand.
win.saga: The Sacred Flame breathed behind a door at the foot of the stair, and you walled it up with rune and stone — some things should stay forgotten.

scenes.title: The Warband That Forgot
scenes.intro: A Warrior-Guild patrol straggles to Runehold's gate, and something is wrong with them. They have forgotten the drill — how a shield-wall locks, which hand holds the spear. The Forgetting, it seems, does not spare steel. Their sergeant asks, ashamed, if the Wizards can put the knowing back into their heads.

advisors.text: So it takes the Warriors too, Archon. If even the drill can be forgotten, no craft is safe — magic or steel. Help them, and they will not forget who did.
Let steel learn how it feels. Why spend our lore teaching men to kill us better?

choices.label: Work a rune of remembering for them

win.text: You set the drill back into their hands with a working, and the shield-wall locks true again. The Guild will remember the debt — and the omen.
win.saga: A Warrior patrol came to your gate with the drill forgotten from their heads, and you worked a rune of remembering — the shield-wall locked true, and the Guild remembered the debt.

choices.label: Send them off with their forgetting

win.text: You turn the patrol away, half-armed and half-remembering. Word spreads that Runehold would not help — and that the Forgetting has begun to eat the Warriors too.
win.saga: A Warrior patrol came with the drill forgotten from their heads, and you sent them off unhelped — word spread that the Forgetting eats steel too, and that Runehold would not help.

scenes.title: The Green Envoy
scenes.intro: An envoy of the Green Wizard City arrives trailing the smell of green things growing, and offers Runehold a blessing on its fields — the old nurture-craft — in the name of friendship between hearth and jungle.

advisors.text: The Green do not offer their craft to just anyone, Archon. Take the blessing, and take the friendship it carries.

choices.label: Let the Green bless the fields

win.text: The envoy walks your furrows murmuring, and by Highsun the fields come up thick and green. The Green City counts Runehold a friend.
win.saga: You let a Green envoy bless Runehold's fields; the harvest came up thick, and the Green City counted you a friend.

choices.label: Trade lore for their growing-craft

win.text: Your Green-craft is enough to learn the rite as they work it — a blessing this year, and the knowing for every year after.
win.saga: You learned the Green growing-rite as their envoy worked it, warming the Green City and enriching your fields.

lose.text: The rite is past your coven's Green-craft to hold, but the envoy blesses the fields anyway, kindly enough.
lose.saga: You could not learn the Green rite, but their envoy blessed Runehold's fields regardless, warming the Green City.

choices.label: Decline — Runehold tends its own soil

win.text: You thank the envoy and send them home. The coven's pride in its own hands is worth a little coolness with the jungle.
win.saga: You declined the Green's blessing to tend Runehold's own soil, holding the coven's pride over the jungle's goodwill.

scenes.title: The Hungry Season
scenes.intro: The stores run thin before the Highsun harvest. The coven looks to you with hollow eyes.

advisors.text: The Warriors of the eastern road carry full wagons. We are Wizards — let us not starve like sheep.
Raiding the Warrior's Guild is how feuds begin. Buy grain. We can afford pride or we can afford winter, not both.

choices.label: Raid a Warrior caravan for grain

win.text: The raid succeeds; wagons roll into Runehold heavy with grain.
win.saga: With the stores thin before harvest, you raided a Warrior caravan for grain — the wagons rolled into Runehold heavy.

lose.text: The Warriors were ready for you. You return with wounded and little else.
lose.saga: With the stores thin before harvest, you raided a Warrior caravan — but they were ready, and you returned with wounded and little else.

choices.label: Trade coin for grain

win.text: A fair price, fairly paid. The stores fill, and no blood is owed.
win.saga: With the stores thin before harvest, you traded coin for grain — the stores filled, and no blood was owed.

scenes.title: The River-Meadow Dispute
scenes.intro: A green river-meadow at Runehold's edge falls into contest: Red merchants want it for a trade-wharf, Green wardens for a water-meadow left wild. Both press their case with gifts and grievances, and both watch to see which colour you favour.

advisors.text: A wharf means coin, Archon — a road for Red gold straight to our gate. Meadows do not fill a granary in a hard year.
That meadow feeds the whole valley's soil. Sell it to Red and you sell a hundred harvests for one season's silver.

choices.label: Favour Red's wharf — coin and trade

win.text: The wharf goes up and Red gold flows over it, and the Gilded Reach counts Runehold a partner — while the Green mark the wild meadow drowned in silver.
win.saga: You gave the river-meadow to Red for a trade-wharf, gaining coin and the Reach's favour and the Green's resentment.

choices.label: Favour Green's water-meadow — the wild and the harvest

win.text: The meadow stays wild and the valley's soil thanks you for it, and the Green count Runehold a friend — while Red pockets its gold and its grudge.
win.saga: You kept the river-meadow wild for the Green, gaining harvest and the jungle's favour and Red's cold ledger.

choices.label: Divide the meadow and anger both a little

win.text: You give each half its half. Neither is happy — a wharf too small, a meadow too shrunk — but neither can call you a partisan.
win.saga: You split the river-meadow between Red and Green; both grumbled at the half they got, but neither named you an enemy.

scenes.title: The Last True Name
scenes.intro: Piece by piece, the failure-tales have begun to rhyme. In every one, a great Wizard reaches, and the memory goes out beneath them at the worst moment. Tonight your eldest reader closes the last book with a shaking hand and says what none of you have dared: "It is not that we grow weak. The world is forgetting how magic is done — and it began when the Flame was broken. The Flame was never only holy, Archon. It was memory. It was how the world remembered magic at all."

advisors.text: Then every Ember is a shard of the world's memory, and Runehold holds a piece of the only thing that can hold the Forgetting back. Gather them, and we do not merely survive — we remember.
If that's true, the ones who broke the Flame doomed us all to forget, to keep it from an enemy. I would like to know who that enemy was — and whether it is still hunting the shards.

choices.label: Set it down in plain words: the Flame is the world's memory

win.text: You write the truth into the coven's book at last, and the whole shape of your saga changes. Runehold is not merely a hold to keep alive — it is a keeper of memory, and the Embers are the road back from the long forgetting.
win.saga: Your eldest reader named the truth at last — the Flame was the world's memory of magic — and you set it in the coven's book: Runehold is a keeper of memory, and the Embers the road back.

scenes.title: The Ley-Tide
scenes.intro: As the season turns, a tide of ley-energy wells up beneath Runehold and the rune-stones begin to hum of their own accord. For a few days the gathering will be easy — magic all but pooling in the palm.

advisors.text: The land is giving freely, Archon. Take what it offers now — a ley-tide does not wait on our convenience.

choices.label: Gather all you can while it lasts

win.text: The coven works the rune-stones day and night, and the mana pools deep before the tide ebbs.
win.saga: A ley-tide welled up beneath Runehold, and you gathered its mana deep before it ebbed.

choices.label: Gather slowly, and chart the flows

win.text: You take less, but your scribes map the ley-lines as they run — a little power now, and a little knowing for later.
win.saga: You gathered the ley-tide slowly and charted its flows — a little mana, and a little lore besides.

scenes.title: The Lingering Soul
scenes.intro: A Forgotten Soul lingers at the edge of Runehold's fields, dim and slow, trailing residual magic like smoke. It could be drawn on — the old, dark art of drinking the power a Soul still carries. Or it could be named, and released.

advisors.text: It is halfway gone already, Archon. What it carries is going to waste. Better in our reserves than drifting on the wind.
That was a Wizard once. Drink from it and the coven will not meet your eye for a season. Let it go with its name.

choices.label: Draw the residual magic from the Soul

win.text: You take what the Soul still holds. The reserves swell — and something goes quiet and cold in the coven that will not warm for a while.
win.saga: You drank the residual magic from a lingering Forgotten Soul; the reserves swelled, and the coven's faith went cold.

choices.label: Speak its name and let it go

win.text: You find the name still in it and speak it aloud. The Soul unravels gently, leaving a last thin gift of mana and a coven the prouder for the mercy.
win.saga: You named a lingering Soul and released it; it left a parting gift of mana, and the coven took pride in the mercy.

scenes.title: The Meditative Season
scenes.intro: A quiet season lies ahead, with no crisis pressing. The Circle could give it over to deep meditation — a slow, disciplined gathering of mana and mastery — though a season spent in trance is a season not spent in the fields.

advisors.text: Let us go deep, Archon. Power gathered in stillness is power that stays. The granary can bear one lean season for it.

choices.label: Give the season to deep meditation

win.text: The Circle sinks into a season-long trance and rises with reserves brimming and their craft honed — though the fields lay half-tended.
win.saga: The Circle gave a whole season to deep meditation, rising with brimming mana and sharpened craft, the fields left lean.

choices.label: A little study between the season's work

win.text: The coven studies in the evenings and works by day. A modest gathering, and the granary untroubled.
win.saga: The coven studied lightly between the season's work, gathering a modest measure of mana.

scenes.title: The Old Archon's Kin
scenes.intro: A Warrior comes to Runehold alone and unarmed, and asks for you by the name of the one you took the seat from. She is the old Archon's sister, and she has brought steel-kin to the treeline behind her. "You made my brother a Forgotten Soul for a chair by a fire," she says. "Give me a reason not to make you one."

advisors.text: This was always going to come for us, Archon. What we did in the taking, we answer for now. Speak carefully.
Grief can be turned, if you are honest with it. Offer her a truth, or a place, before you offer her a fight.

choices.label: Offer her a place in the Circle, and the truth

win.text: You tell her plainly what you did and why, and offer her brother's seat at the Circle rather than his grave. It is not forgiveness — but she stays, and the steel goes home.
win.saga: The old Archon's sister came with steel-kin for the seat you took by force; you offered her the truth and a place in the Circle, and the steel went home.

lose.text: The words ring hollow even as you say them. She spits at the hearth-rune and rides, and the treeline empties — for now. The debt is not paid.
lose.saga: The old Archon's sister came for the seat you took by force; your words of atonement rang hollow, and she rode off with the debt unpaid.

choices.label: Tell her the seat was taken and will be kept

win.text: You give her no ground. She nods, almost respecting it, and promises to return with more than words. Runehold has an enemy with a name now.
win.saga: The old Archon's sister came for the seat you took by force, and you gave her no ground — she promised to return with more than words, an enemy with a name.

scenes.title: The Purple Pavilion's Dare
scenes.intro: Purple wizards arrive on a wind that smells of ozone and set up a revel of wild, unleashed workings on Runehold's green — and dare the coven to join them, or to send a champion to their working-contest. The chaos is catching. So is the Purple's regard, for those who dare.

advisors.text: The Purple respect one thing, Archon — nerve. Join them, contest them, but do not stand at the edge and disapprove. That, they never forgive.
Or send them off with their revel. Chaos courted is chaos let in, and the White will think the better of us for the door we keep shut.

choices.label: Join the Purple revel of wild workings

win.text: The coven throws itself into the wild working and comes away crackling, laughing, a little singed — and firm friends of the Pavilion.
win.saga: The coven joined the Purple's revel of wild workings, winning the Pavilion's friendship and a little raw mana besides.

choices.label: Meet their champion in a working-contest

win.text: Your champion out-chaoses their chaos, and the Pavilion roars its approval. Runehold has bragging rights and a doting Purple both.
win.saga: Your champion beat the Purple's own at their working-contest, winning the Pavilion's loud and lasting regard.

lose.text: Your champion is out-matched and half-unmade in the trying. The Purple love a game try, but the wild backlash scars the hold.
lose.saga: Your champion lost the Purple's working-contest and took a wild backlash home, though the Pavilion warmed to the daring.

choices.label: Send the revel on its way

win.text: You bar the green to them, politely. The Purple leave unimpressed — but word of your restraint reaches the White Tower, and it approves.
win.saga: You refused the Purple's revel; the Pavilion cooled toward Runehold, but the orderly White warmed to your restraint.

scenes.title: The Relic Battery
scenes.intro: A trader leaves a strange thing in pawn: a pre-Burning relic of rune-etched brass, still faintly warm — a battery, the coven decides, built to store a great charge of mana. Getting the charge out without ruining the relic is a matter of the old craft.

advisors.text: It is good work, Archon — old, practical, and full. Coax it right and it pours. Force it and we get a fistful and a cracked shell.

choices.label: Unlock the relic's stored charge

win.text: Your craft is equal to the old maker's. The relic gives up its whole charge in a clean, gorgeous flood, and survives to be charged again.
win.saga: The coven's craft unlocked a pre-Burning mana-battery cleanly; it poured out its whole charge and survived whole.

lose.text: The old bindings are subtler than they looked. The relic cracks as it discharges — a fair draught of mana, and a ruined, worthless shell.
lose.saga: The coven cracked a pre-Burning mana-battery drawing its charge; some mana came, and a priceless relic was ruined.

choices.label: Study it a season before touching it

win.text: You leave the charge alone and study the relic's making instead — a season's careful notes, and a little mana teased safely from its surface.
win.saga: You studied a pre-Burning mana-battery for a season before daring its charge, gaining lore and a little mana.

scenes.title: The Restless Wizard
scenes.intro: One of the Circle comes to you at the hearth with a road already in their eyes. There is a thread of old lore, they say — a rumour of a working the world has forgotten — and it runs out of Runehold and into the ash. They ask your leave to follow it alone, though it may take the better part of a year, and the road is not kind to those who walk it far.

advisors.text: Let them go, Archon. A Wizard with a road in their eyes is no use kept behind walls — and they may come home with something worth the year.
The far road takes as often as it gives. Give your leave with open eyes: not everyone who walks that far comes home as they went.

choices.label: Give your leave — let them follow the thread

win.text: You clasp the Wizard's arm and let them go. They walk out the gate at dawn with a light pack and a long road, and Runehold is one Wizard fewer until they return — if they return.
win.saga: You gave one of the Circle leave to walk the long road alone after a thread of forgotten lore.

choices.label: Refuse — the Circle keeps its own

win.text: You tell them the Circle is too few to lose even one to a rumour. They bow to it, but the road stays in their eyes, and something in them cools toward you.
win.saga: You refused a restless Wizard leave to walk the long road; they obeyed, but it cost you something with them.

scenes.title: The Rite Disputed
scenes.intro: A rite your coven recovered turns out to be claimed by two cults at once: the Purple say it is chaos-work and theirs by blood, the White that it is an ordering-rite and theirs by right. Both have sent for it, and neither will take a refusal warmly.

advisors.text: It is Purple to the bone, Archon — read how it bends. Give it to the Pavilion and make a friend who dances. Give it to the White and make one who audits.
It is an ordering-rite dressed as chaos. The White Tower will owe us for its return. The Purple will forget the slight by next season, as they forget everything.

choices.label: Give the rite to the Purple Pavilion

win.text: The Purple carry the rite off in delight and count Runehold kin — while the White Tower files the loss in its long, cool memory.
win.saga: You gave the disputed rite to the Purple Pavilion, winning its delight and the White Tower's quiet displeasure.

choices.label: Give the rite to the White Tower

win.text: The White receive the rite with grave thanks and a debt acknowledged — while the Purple shrug, and remember, in their way.
win.saga: You gave the disputed rite to the White Tower, earning its gratitude and a debt, and the Purple's careless grudge.

choices.label: Keep the rite for Runehold

win.text: You make the case that a rite recovered is a rite kept, and make it so well that both cults grumble and grant it. Runehold keeps its find.
win.saga: You argued Runehold's right to keep the disputed rite so ably that both Purple and White grumbled and let it lie.

lose.text: Your claim convinces no one, and now both cults are sure Runehold is a hoarder. You keep the rite and lose the goodwill.
lose.saga: Your claim to keep the disputed rite convinced neither cult; Runehold kept its find and lost face with Purple and White both.

scenes.title: The Rival Hearth
scenes.intro: This hearth still burns. A hold no larger than Runehold keeps its Ember behind a stone door and a wary Circle of its own, and its Archon meets you on the threshold with a working half-shaped in her hand. "We know what you carry," she says. "We know what you want. Two Embers are stronger than one — but not if one hold swallows the other."

advisors.text: Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole. Treat with them, Archon. This is how the Flame comes home — not by conquest.
Or take theirs. One great fire, in one strong hold, is worth more than two frightened ones. They would do the same to us, given the nerve.

choices.label: Swear an accord between the two hearths

win.text: You clasp hands over both hearth-runes, and for a moment the two Embers lean toward each other like they remember being one Flame. An accord is sworn: not a gathering yet, but the first thread of one.
win.saga: You met another living Ember behind a stone door, and swore an accord between the two hearths — the first thread of a gathering.

lose.text: The words come out too grasping, and the other Archon's door closes in your face. You have not made an enemy — but you have not made a friend, and the road home is long.
lose.saga: You sought an accord with a rival hearth's Circle, but grasped too openly, and its Archon's door closed in your face.

choices.label: Take their Ember by force of magic

win.text: It is quick, and it is ugly. You leave with two Embers where you came with one — and with the certain knowledge that every other hearth will now bar its door to Runehold, and sharpen something for you.
win.saga: You took a rival hold's Ember by force of magic — two Embers where you came with one, and every other hearth now barred against Runehold.

lose.text: Their Circle was ready, and yours was not. You are driven off with nothing, and word will run ahead of you: Runehold reaches for other holds' fires. That name will cost you.
lose.saga: You reached for a rival hold's Ember by force, but their Circle was ready and yours was not — driven off, with word running ahead that Runehold reaches for others' fires.

scenes.title: The Rune-Node
scenes.intro: Diggers strike an old rune-node beneath the hold — a knot of bound magic older than Runehold, still holding its charge. Tapping it cleanly is a matter of order and precise wards; forced, it will give all at once, one way or another.

advisors.text: A node like this answers to order, Archon. With clean wards it will pour for us for a season. Without them, it will simply pour.

choices.label: Read its wards and tap it cleanly

win.text: Your order-craft is equal to it. The node opens like a tap and gives its charge in a steady, safe stream.
win.saga: The coven's order-craft opened an ancient rune-node cleanly, and it gave up its stored mana in a safe stream.

choices.label: Force the node open

win.text: You crack its bindings by main lore and catch most of the charge before it earths itself. Rough, but it worked.
win.saga: You forced an old rune-node open by lore and caught most of its charge before it discharged.

lose.text: The node lets go all at once and earths itself through the hold's stones — a jolt of mana, and a jagged new stress in the Fracture.
lose.saga: A forced rune-node discharged through Runehold's stones — some mana caught, and the Fracture stressed wider.

scenes.title: The Sealed Stair
scenes.intro: Something scratches behind the sealed door, patient and unhurried. The mortar is cold to the touch, and getting colder.

advisors.text: It remembers that we shut it out. Walls have a way of becoming doors again.

choices.label: Reinforce the seal with deeper runes

win.text: You bind the stair with deeper wards. The scratching stops — for now.
win.saga: Something scratched behind the sealed door at the foot of the stair, and you bound it with deeper wards — the scratching stopped, for now.

choices.label: Press your ear to the stone and listen

win.text: For a single heartbeat, a voice you knew. Then nothing. The Flame's Regard stirs in your chest.
win.saga: You pressed your ear to the sealed stair and heard, for a heartbeat, a voice you knew — and the Flame's Regard stirred in your chest.

scenes.title: The Seam Beckons
scenes.intro: A shepherd stumbles in from the high ground raving about cold light: a Fracture-seam has opened in the hills, wide and deep and quiet, and it does not close. Your readers grow still when they hear where. Down in a seam like that, the oldest of them says, may lie fragments of the world's first memory — how magic was done before the Flame broke. To go down is to risk two Wizards to a thing that eats minds. To leave it is to leave the deepest well of lore in the world unread.

advisors.text: We will not find its like again, Archon. A living Fracture-seam is a wound in the world that remembers what the world has forgotten. Send our steadiest minds down, carefully, and we may read the road home from the Forgetting itself.
Or seal it and be glad of the walls. A seam that eats memory does not care how clever the mind we send it. Some wells are meant to stay unread.

choices.label: Send a party down into the seam

win.text: You choose two of the Circle steady enough to look into the deep and come back knowing their own names, and send them to the seam in the hills. What they read there, if they read at all, may change everything.
win.saga: You sent a party down into a living Fracture-seam to read the fragments of the world's first memory.

choices.label: Leave the seam unread

win.text: You give the order to ward the hills and leave the seam alone. Whatever the deep remembers, it can keep — Runehold cannot spend two minds on the chance of reading it.
win.saga: You left the Fracture-seam in the hills unread, warding it rather than risk two Wizards to the deep.

scenes.title: The Tourney is Called
scenes.intro: A herald rides the circuit of the holds: the champion's tourney is called again in the tourney-town, a purse of coin and a season's glory to whichever hearth or guild sends the last one standing. A small hold could make a great name — if it had a champion to send, and could spare that Wizard the season.

advisors.text: Send our best, Archon. A win at the lists is worth a hundred fair words about Runehold — every hold that sneers at us would learn our champion's name.
Or send no one and keep our pride whole. A champion who loses brings home a story we'd rather not tell.

choices.label: Send a champion to the lists

win.text: You choose your strongest hand and send them south to the tourney-town with a purse for the road. Runehold has entered the lists.
win.saga: You sent a champion of the Circle south to fight in the champion's tourney for Runehold's name.

choices.label: Send no one — glory is not grain

win.text: You let the herald ride on. Runehold's name will not ring at the lists this year; nor will one of your own be gone a season chasing a crown.
win.saga: You let the champion's tourney pass, keeping your Wizards home rather than chase a season's glory.

scenes.title: The Tower That Forgot
scenes.intro: A trader from the Bastion swaps you a Story for a sack of grain: the tale of the White wizard Serevane, who bound a fortress to walk on stone legs and carry her people out of the Fracture's path. It walked for a year and a day. Then, one still morning, it forgot how a wall holds up a roof — and came down all at once, with everyone she had meant to save still inside.

advisors.text: Order is the first thing the Forgetting takes, Archon. Serevane's fortress did not break — it lost the memory of being whole. As may we all.
One story, told for grain. It may be true; it may be a Bastion's bad dream dressed as history. Weigh it — don't kneel to it.

choices.label: Record the Story in the coven's own book

win.text: Serevane's walking fortress joins your slow-growing library of the ways magic has failed. A grim shelf — and the truest map you own of the road the whole world is walking.
win.saga: You traded grain for the Story of Serevane, whose walking fortress forgot how a wall holds a roof and fell — and set it in the coven's book of magic's failures.

choices.label: Trade the Story onward for coin

win.text: A grim tale sells as well as a glad one. You pass Serevane's fall along the market roads and pocket the difference.
win.saga: You took the Story of Serevane's fallen walking fortress and traded it onward for coin — a grim tale sells as well as a glad one.

lose.text: No one wants to buy an omen this season. The Story stays with you, heavier for having tried to sell it.
lose.saga: You tried to sell on the Story of Serevane's fallen fortress, but no one wanted an omen that season, and it stayed with you, heavier.

scenes.title: The Trembling Ground
scenes.intro: At Deepfrost the ground shudders and a seam of cold light splits the hillside above Runehold — the Fracture, surfacing. Through the crack, something the colour of nothing turns over in its sleep.

advisors.text: The old wards can close it, but the working will cost us. Better cost than catastrophe.
Or we go down and look. Whatever sleeps there knows things the Flame itself forgot.

choices.label: Pour mana and lore into sealing the seam

win.text: Apprentices chant until their voices fail; the seam knits shut and the light goes out. The hill is whole, and so are you — barely.
win.saga: At Deepfrost the Fracture split the hillside above Runehold, and you poured mana and lore into sealing the seam until the light went out.

choices.label: Descend into the seam to learn what sleeps there

win.text: You return wide-eyed with a fragment of the world's first memory — and the seam, satisfied, closes behind you.
win.saga: When the Fracture split the hill above Runehold, you descended into the seam and returned with a fragment of the world's first memory, the crack closing behind you.

lose.text: What you find down there finds you back. You climb out changed, and the seam stays open, breathing.
lose.saga: You descended into the Fracture's seam above Runehold and what you found found you back — you climbed out changed, and the seam stayed open, breathing.

scenes.title: The Wanderer in the Ash
scenes.intro: A figure walks out of the ash-haze at dusk and stops at the edge of the firelight. A Forgotten Soul — robes you almost recognize, a face the Flame has rewritten. It does not speak. It waits to be known.

advisors.text: It came to us. The Flame sends nothing by accident. Let it in.
I have seen what wears a familiar face and means us harm. Keep the gate shut, Archon.

choices.label: Welcome the Soul to the fire

win.text: It sits among you and, in a voice like settling embers, gives up a rune the living had lost. By dawn it is gone, but the knowledge stays.
win.saga: A Forgotten Soul waited at the edge of the firelight, and you welcomed it in — it gave up a lost rune in a voice like settling embers, and was gone by dawn.

lose.text: Whatever sat at your fire that night left something wrong behind it. The coven sleeps badly for a long while.
lose.saga: You welcomed a Forgotten Soul to the fire, and it left something wrong behind it — the coven slept badly for a long while.

choices.label: Turn the Soul away into the dark

win.text: You bar the gate. It stands a long moment, then walks back into the ash. The coven breathes easier; you do not.
win.saga: A Forgotten Soul waited at the edge of the firelight to be known, and you barred the gate — it walked back into the ash, and you did not breathe easier.

scenes.title: The Wandering Mystic
scenes.intro: A hooded mystic comes to Runehold's gate — no cult's colour, no coven's mark — and offers to teach the coven an old rite for drawing mana out of still air. The rite, she warns, asks a steady and believing hand; a wavering one it punishes.

advisors.text: Free knowledge from a stranger is rarely free, Archon — but if the rite is true, it is ours to keep. Weigh whether our discipline is equal to it.

choices.label: Learn the mystic's mana-rite

win.text: The coven takes the rite to heart, and by the third night mana rises from empty air at a word. A gift worth the risk.
win.saga: A wandering mystic taught the coven a rite to draw mana from still air, and your faith was steady enough to hold it.

lose.text: The rite asks more steadiness than the coven can muster, and it unravels in their hands — days wasted, and confidence with them.
lose.saga: The mystic's mana-rite unravelled in unsteady hands, and the coven lost days and heart to it.

choices.label: Pay her to gather for you instead

win.text: You pay the mystic's price and she works alone for a season, leaving your reserves fuller and asking nothing more.
win.saga: You paid the wandering mystic to gather mana for a season rather than risk the rite yourselves.

scenes.title: The Warrior-Guild's Levy
scenes.intro: Riders of the Warrior-Guild draw up before Runehold's gate, banners stiff in the cold. Their captain does not dismount. "The roads are ours to keep, and keeping costs," she calls. "Grain, or coin, or the loan of a Wizard's fire for the season. Choose."

advisors.text: They stood with us once, Archon. Give freely, and for once the debt runs the other way.
They remember the day you struck first, and they have come to make you pay for it in grain. Give nothing you don't have to.
Every road out of this costs. Coin we can spare; grain we cannot; a Wizard's fire, never lightly.

choices.label: Pay in grain

win.text: The wagons roll out heavy, and the riders let Runehold's roads alone another season.
win.saga: The Warrior-Guild's captain demanded a levy for keeping the roads, and you paid in grain — the riders let Runehold's roads alone another season.

choices.label: Pay in coin

win.text: Coin changes hands, and the captain touches her helm in something like courtesy before she rides.
win.saga: The Warrior-Guild demanded a levy for the roads, and you paid in coin — the captain touched her helm in something like courtesy.

choices.label: Refuse the levy

win.text: You meet the captain's eye and hold it, and something in your bearing makes the price not worth the taking. She rides — this time.
win.saga: The Warrior-Guild demanded a levy for the roads, and you refused — something in your bearing made the price not worth the taking, this time.

lose.text: Your refusal lands as insult. The riders take what they came for and a little more, and the roads grow meaner toward Runehold.
lose.saga: You refused the Warrior-Guild's levy, and it landed as insult — the riders took what they came for and more, and the roads grew meaner toward Runehold.

scenes.title: The White Tower's Audit
scenes.intro: Inspectors of the White Wizard Tower arrive unasked, with instruments and a cool courtesy, to audit Runehold's wards against the Fracture. Order is their gift and their judgement; how you receive them will set the Tower's word on you for years.

advisors.text: Let them look, Archon. Our wards are sound, and the White Tower's blessing is worth more than a season's pride. Show them everything.
Or show them the gate. Runehold answers to the Flame, not to the Tower's clipboards. Let them audit their own walls.

choices.label: Welcome the inspectors and open the wards to them

win.text: You open every ward to their instruments. They find them sound, tighten two, and leave a Tower's approval and a steadier hold behind.
win.saga: You welcomed the White Tower's audit; they judged Runehold's wards sound, tightening them and warming the Tower to you.

choices.label: Show them wards worthy of the Tower itself

win.text: Your order-craft startles even the Tower's own. They leave calling Runehold a model hold, and mean it.
win.saga: Your order-craft so impressed the White Tower's inspectors that they named Runehold a model hold.

lose.text: Your wards are honest but plain, and the inspectors note every fault in their cool hand. Sound enough — hardly a model.
lose.saga: The White Tower's inspectors found Runehold's wards plain but sound, warming to you only a little.

choices.label: Rebuff the audit — Runehold answers to itself

win.text: You send the inspectors back to their Tower unsatisfied. Runehold keeps its pride and its privacy — and loses a powerful friend it never quite had.
win.saga: You rebuffed the White Tower's audit; Runehold kept its independence and lost the Tower's regard for it.

scenes.title: Word of a Living Hearth
scenes.intro: A trader with frightened eyes brings you more than goods: word of another living hearth, a hold to the south that keeps its own Ember behind a stone door and a wary Circle. "They know your fire burns too," he murmurs. "They wonder if you'll come as a friend — or as the one who takes their fire for your own." It is a season's road, there and back, and it would strip two Wizards from the Circle while they walk it.

advisors.text: This is how the Flame comes home, Archon — hearth by hearth, thread by thread. Send an embassy, and send it in good faith. Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole.
Or send an embassy that keeps its options open. If they will treat, treat. If they are weak, we now know where a second Ember sleeps, and who guards it.

choices.label: Send a pilgrimage to the rival hearth

win.text: You choose who will carry Runehold's name south, and set them on the long road to a stranger's fire. Whether they come home with an ally or an Ember, only the season will tell.
win.saga: You sent a pilgrimage south to the rival hearth that keeps its own living Ember.

choices.label: Leave the other hearth to its own fire

win.text: You send the trader on with a full belly and no answer. Let the southern hold keep its fire and its secrets; Runehold has road enough to walk without adding another.
win.saga: You let word of the rival living hearth pass, leaving the southern hold to its own fire.

scenes.title: Yellow's Warning
scenes.intro: Word arrives from the Chronomancer's Riviera unbidden: their sightings show a hard turn coming for Runehold, and they have sent the warning freely — hoping, plainly, that a favour given is a favour returned.

advisors.text: They warned us for nothing, Archon. Honour it. A cult that does us a kindness should not find us ungrateful.

choices.label: Heed the warning and prepare

win.text: You spend to shore up against what they foresaw, and send the Riviera your thanks in coin. The trouble, when it comes, breaks softer.
win.saga: You heeded Yellow's freely-given warning and repaid it, easing the coming trouble and binding the Riviera closer.

choices.label: Take the warning, thank them, pay nothing

win.text: You act on the warning but send no gift. The Riviera notes both — that you listened, and that you gave nothing back.
win.saga: You acted on Yellow's warning but sent no thanks, easing the trouble while leaving the Riviera cool.

choices.label: Dismiss the chronomancers' fear

win.text: You set the warning aside as Yellow fretting. Perhaps it was. The Riviera will remember that you did not listen.
win.saga: You dismissed Yellow's warning as idle fretting, souring Runehold's standing with the Riviera.

scenes.title: Ash on the Wind
scenes.intro: Grey ash drifts in from the east for three days running. The old ones say the Fracture is breathing again.

choices.label: Record it in the saga and move on

win.text: You note the omen. The coven mutters and watches the horizon.
win.saga: Grey ash drifted from the east for three days, and you recorded the omen — the coven muttered and watched the horizon.

scenes.title: The Long Dark
scenes.intro: The sun comes up wrong three mornings running — dim, and a finger's width south of where it should be. Across the Runiverse, holds light their hearths early and speak in low voices.

choices.label: Walk among the coven and steady them

win.text: You make the rounds, hearth to hearth, until the muttering settles into resolve.
win.saga: The sun rose wrong and dim three mornings running, and you walked the coven hearth to hearth until the muttering settled into resolve.

choices.label: Record the omen and let it pass

win.text: You set it down in the saga. The dark does not lift, but it does not deepen.
win.saga: The sun rose wrong three mornings running, and you set the omen in the saga and let it pass — the dark did not lift, nor deepen.

circle.name: Ember
circle.bio: Young, reckless, and drawn to the Sacred Flame like a moth to its namesake. The Circle worries about her. She does not.

circle.name: Korr Ashen
circle.bio: Survived the Great Burning with the scars to prove it. Has buried more Souls than apprentices, and will tell you so unprompted.

circle.name: Vela
circle.bio: Keeper of Runehold's half-burned Athenaeum-scrolls. Believes memory is the only wealth the cataclysm cannot take, and argues it at every Circle.

circle.name: Wisp
circle.bio: Quiet and devout, already half-listening to something the others cannot hear. Speaks of the Flame as though it has spoken to him first.

codex.title: Introduction
codex.blurb: The world you inherit, and the fire you keep.

entries.body: Once the Sacred Flame was tended whole in the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. Then its keepers vanished, the Tower went dark — and the world began, slowly, to forget. Magic thins, runes slip from memory, and the great powers of the old age have dwindled to a scatter of guttering hearths in the dark.

You are the appointed Archon of Runehold — though who named you, and why, is itself half-forgotten. As the Tower's enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into Embers and scattered them beyond reach. One was carried to your hearth-rune: the coven's heart, and its last thread to the old power. Let it die, and Runehold dies with it.

Beyond your walls the seven Colour Cults circle — each hoarding what magic remains, and no two of them allies for long. Win one to your side and you make an enemy of another. There is no pleasing all seven.

Your Wizards will counsel you, and quarrel. Famine, feud, and worse are coming. Lead them through it — and, if the old stories are true, seek the other Embers before the dark finds them first. Keep the flame, or be forgotten.

codex.title: The Pressures
codex.blurb: Seven meters and your standing with the Cults. Every scene reads and writes them.

entries.body: Your spellcasting reserve. Powers workings, quests, and defenses. Regenerates slowly, spent in bursts.
Food for the coven in a broken world. Famine is a constant low threat; a coven cannot eat runes.
The mercantile axis, ruled by the Gilded Reach and the Red Cult. Buys what magic and farming cannot — and ties you to aristocrat politics.
Runes and memory: the signature meter. Unlocks great workings, but DECAYS every season — 'The Forgetting' made mechanical. Maintain libraries, train apprentices, or watch power slip away.
The coven's morale — whether your Wizards believe you can lead them through the dark. Driven by Circle relationships and consistency with your values.

The cosmic meter. Drawing on the Sacred Flame is the highest reward and the highest risk: salvation, transformation, or undesirable loss. VEILED by default — you feel its pull through what your choices cost, but the running number is hidden. Reveal it under Options → Display.

The doom track. The cataclysm is not finished — Imps, stirring Beasts, and the slow Forgetting advance it. Reaches its limit and Runehold is consumed. VEILED by default — read its mood on the Map (quiet → stirring → rising → dire) rather than a number; reveal the exact meter under Options → Display.

codex.title: The Seven Color Cults
codex.blurb: Seven independent reputations. Acting for one often costs another.

entries.body: The Gilded Reach — aristocrats and coin-mages of the Red Wizard Capital, keepers of the Stock Sanctuary. The mercantile and political power of the Runiverse: court them for wealth, and expect to pay for everything.

The Bastion — keepers of the largest libraries and the Forgotten Athenaeum, where science and magic are one study. The lore and knowledge power, and the surest hand against the Forgetting — if you can meet their price in patience.

The chronomancers of the Riviera, whose magic bends time itself — hourglasses, second chances, and debts that fall due out of order. Bright and mercurial; a Yellow favour may arrive before you ask for it, or long after it could have helped.

The practical cult of the northern delta, workers of plain Orange Magic. No towers and no airs — mud, craft, and things that simply work. Provincial and stubborn, they are the Runiverse's hands, and they remember who kept them fed.

The wild cult of the Green Wizard City, deep in the equatorial jungle. Their magic is growth and rot and living things — rooted, patient, faintly feral. They judge a coven by what it takes from the land, not what it raises upon it.

The chaos-cult of the Purple Pavilion, on its far western island. Esoteric and unstable, they court the loud and forbidden edges of magic — brilliant allies whose gifts are real and never quite controllable.

The order-cult of the White Wizard Tower, keepers of clarity and law. Quiet, austere, and certain, they prize a still hand and a kept oath — and they watch the Sacred Flame more closely, and more warily, than any other colour.

codex.title: Powers & Perils
codex.blurb: The forces your saga is written against.

entries.body: Once tended whole in the Secret Tower, the greatest power and the greatest loss in the world.
