When the Tower's keepers vanished and its enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into EMBERS and scattered them to hidden hearths rather than let it be seized. Draw on an Ember and you may work great magic — but Wizards who step too far into it become Forgotten Souls (roughly one in ten, something undesirable).

The shards of the Sacred Flame, carried out of the Secret Tower and scattered so no enemy could take the whole. Each is the burning heart of a hearth like Runehold. How many survived, and where, is unknown — kept, lost, gone cold, or in the wrong hands. Reuniting the Embers is the long road back to re-lighting the Tower. You hold one; there are others to find.

The world is literally forgetting itself — magic thins, runes slip from memory, and once-simple workings unravel. In game terms Lore decays every season unless held. Tomes and Stories the coven recovers point to a single cause: the Forgetting began when the Flame was broken. What that truly means, the wise are only starting to grasp.
The cataclysm that tore the world apart is not finished. It is the long game beneath all the others — the doom that takes Runehold if neglected.

Your advisor ring is rolled fresh each campaign — Wizards, each with a school (colour), a class, a rank, an age, four competence stats (Power, Wisdom, Guile, Courage) and a temperament (Boldness, Piety, Temper). They grow a year older each year — their portrait ages with them. On a scene, whoever fits the moment speaks — and they disagree. Competence decides what they can pull off; temperament decides what they urge. The second-in-command casts your workings by default.

Every Wizard practises one of the seven colour schools (Red, Yellow, Brown, Green, Blue, Purple, White) and holds one of nine classes: Magus, Sorcerer, Druid, Necromancer, Pyromancer, Enchanter, Charmer, Chaos Mage, Ghost Eater. School is the flavour of the magic; class is what they do best. Who stands in your Circle shapes what workings the coven can attempt.

Spells the coven casts on its own initiative, on the Workings screen. Each belongs to a colour school and tests one competence (Power, Wisdom, Guile, or Courage) against a difficulty. You choose the caster — the second-in-command casts by default. A caster whose school matches the working, backed by high mastery, casts at far better odds; on a win the working takes full effect, on a loss a weaker one. Nothing is ever certain.

How deeply the coven commands each of the seven schools (0–100), separate from your standing with the cults. Opening mastery reflects who sits in your Circle — the schools your Wizards practise start strong, the rest near nothing. Casting a working of a school practises it, nudging that mastery up. Higher mastery raises the odds on every working of that colour.
Wizards transformed by the Flame. When a Circle member is taken, the loss is permanent — they leave the Circle but persist in the world as a Soul you may later meet, bargain with, or mourn.

Fragments of the old world the coven recovers — a burned tome, a survivor's account, a cautionary tale of some great Wizard who reached for an epic working and failed catastrophically as the memory drained out from under them. Each fills in the Codex and, piece by piece, the truth of the Flame and the Embers. The Blue Bastion and its Forgotten Athenaeum chase them hardest.

Your coven seat, and one of the scattered hearths — a few dozen souls keeping an EMBER of the Sacred Flame alive in a guttering hearth-rune. It is the coven's heart and its last thread to the old power: let it die and Runehold dies with it. Grow it from a hearth to a power, or dwindle into a Forgotten hold.

Some undertakings cannot be done from the hearth. When you send a party out — to open a tomb, fight at the lists, or follow a thread of lore into the ash — those Wizards LEAVE the Circle for several seasons. While away they cannot cast, counsel, or hold a task, though they keep their place on the roster. The party sends word each season as the quest unfolds, and your choices steer it; then they come home — richer for it, changed by it, or not at all. Choose who goes with care: a strong hand on the road is a strong hand you do without at home.

The far road takes as surely as it gives. A Wizard gone long may return wearing their own face but with the Flame's hand upon them — a Forgotten Soul where a Wizard set out. And a returning Soul is not always the one who left: sometimes it is one of your own, long lost or given to the Flame, come back in a borrowed shape. When such a one waits at the firelight to be known, you must NAME it. Name it true and you grant it a kind of peace; name it wrong, and the Circle will wonder what else you have forgotten.

actions.label: Drive the Harvest
actions.desc: Push the whole coven into the fields from dawn to dark. The granary fills; the spirit frays.

actions.label: Bless the Fields
actions.desc: Coax the crops with growth-runes and a little spent mana.

actions.label: Buy Seed-Grain
actions.desc: Send coin down the market roads for grain and good seed.

actions.label: Forage the Wastes
actions.desc: Send gatherers into the broken lands. There is food out there — and worse than food.

actions.label: Sell Rune-Craft
actions.desc: Send worked charms and minor enchantments down the market roads for coin.

actions.label: Buy Mana-Reagents
actions.desc: Purchase rare salts and ash-glass to replenish the coven's reserve.

actions.label: Trade the Surplus
actions.desc: Sell what the granary can spare while the price is good.

actions.label: Court the Red Factors
actions.desc: Grease the Stock Sanctuary's palms; the Gilded Reach remembers a paying friend.

actions.label: Raid the Caravans
actions.desc: Fall on the trade-roads and take what Runehold needs. The world remembers a raider.

actions.label: Muster the Coven
actions.desc: Call every hand to arms and feed them well; a coven that can fight stands taller.

actions.label: Fortify the Hold
actions.desc: Raise watchfires and deepen the walls against what the Fracture sends.

actions.label: Hire Warrior Mercenaries
actions.desc: Steel you cannot cast walks your walls — and the coven mutters at trusting those who fear the Flame.

actions.label: Mend the Wards
actions.desc: Reinforce the rune-wards of Runehold against the spreading Fracture. A White working — order against decay.

win.text: The wards flare and hold. The Fracture's creep slows against Runehold's stones.

lose.text: The runes gutter half-set. The wards barely hold, and the mana is spent all the same.

actions.label: Deepen the Runes
actions.desc: Chant the old runes deeper into memory, holding back the Forgetting. A Blue working — knowledge against loss.

win.text: The lore sinks in true. The coven's memory deepens against the dark.

lose.text: The chant slips; only fragments take. A little is remembered, most is not.

actions.label: Tend the Hearth-Flame
actions.desc: Keep the hearth-rune bright through the long dark; the coven takes heart. A Green working — nurture against despair.

win.text: The hearth-rune blooms warm and steady. The coven sleeps easier for it.

lose.text: The flame sputters low. It holds, but the warmth does not reach far.

actions.label: Court the Sacred Flame
actions.desc: Draw on the Flame itself — beyond any school. Its regard grows, but drawing deep stirs what should stay still. A test of nerve, not colour.

win.text: The Flame answers, and does not take. Its regard settles on Runehold like a warm weight.

lose.text: The Flame stirs and turns cold. It grants a little regard, but something has woken that should have slept.

coven.text: Faith is the only wall that never falls. Tend the coven's heart before its stones.
Sentiment won't fill a belly. Keep them fed and they'll keep the faith.
A coven grows by daring. Send me where the danger is — the others will follow.
Guard who we have. Every Wizard lost is a rune we never chant again.
Half these apprentices have never bled for the hold. That should change.
Their faith is guttering like the hearth-rune. Mend it now, or we lose them one by one.
The coven would follow you into the Flame itself. Don't spend that trust lightly.
We are what remains of the old Circle. Lead us well, and we hold.

fields.text: The stores won't stretch to the thaw. We plant now, or we starve in it.
Let me lead a forage into the deep wastes. I'll come back heavy, or not at all.
Bless the fields. The Flame has fed us through darker springs than this.
Buy seed-grain while the Reach still trades. Coin keeps better than hope.
Drive the harvest hard. Backs will ache, but bellies won't.
The stores are nearly bare. This is no season for half-measures — we plant or forage now.
We're a bad week from famine. Whatever you mean to do, do it this season.
A coven cannot eat runes. Mind the stores before they mind us.

market.text: Coin is the one magic the Gilded Reach respects. Spend it before it spends us.
Don't owe the Reach a favour. Their debts come due in blood.
I can turn this coin twice before the factors notice. Let me do the dealing.
Trade if we must — but do not kneel to aristocrats who forgot the Flame.
Buy Red's favour now. We'll want a friend with a full purse before long.
The purse is near empty. We've little left to trade our way out of trouble.
Give the coin a job, or it does nothing at all.

war.text: Raid the caravans. Steel fears the magic that strikes first.
Every raid stirs the Fracture. Fortify — don't provoke what we can't unmake.
Hire mercenaries and let them bleed for us. I'd sooner cast than swing a blade.
The coven distrusts steel it cannot enchant. Do not lean on hired swords.
Muster the coven. If the Warriors come, let them find us ready.
The ash is at the walls. Fortify, or the Fracture ends this hold before any Warrior does.
Magic against steel — choose the ground before they choose it for you.

workings.text: My hands are steady. Give me the hard working and stand well back.
Deepen the runes while I still remember them. The Forgetting takes the old spells first.
Court the Flame if you dare — but send someone whose soul you can spare.
Mend the wards first. A working undone is cheaper than a hold unmade.
Match the caster to the colour. Waste no mana on a spell that fights the hand that casts it.
We're all but dry. One working, chosen well — and no more this season.
Mend the wards before anything else. The Fracture is close enough to feel now.
The old runes are slipping from us. Deepen them while any of us still remember them.
Mana sits idle. Put it to work before it's needed and gone.

cults.text: Choose your friends among the seven with care. Every ally is another's enemy.
A cult wronged remembers longer than a cult befriended. Mind your feuds.
Play the colours against each other. We're too small to fight them, big enough to tip them.
The Blue Bastion still keeps the old lore. Court them before the Forgetting wins it.
Let them circle. A hold that flinches at every cult never grows.
Seven cults, and no pleasing all of them at once. Choose.

map.text: The Secret Tower waits out there in the dark. Someone must dare the road to it.
Every hold on that map can become a grave. Scout before you stride.
All roads bend back to the Tower and its Flame. That is the only road that matters.
The world forgets itself a little more each season. Map it while it still holds a shape.
Look how the ash has eaten the edges. Soon there'll be no map left to read.
The Flame leans toward us. The Tower is nearer now than it has ever been — press on.
Runehold at the centre, the ash at the edge. Watch which of them grows.

saga.text: What we do is written whether we will it or not. Give the chroniclers worthy ink.
The saga keeps no lies. Read it when you doubt the road we've walked.
Let them write that Runehold dared. Better a bright saga than a long grey one.
If this is to be our last chapter, let it read that we did not go quietly.
Every choice becomes a line in this. Make it a saga worth the telling.

steps.title: Keeper of the Sacred Flame
body: There was an age when the Sacred Flame burned whole beneath the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. But the keepers of that fire went out from the Tower and did not return; the great door fell dark; and slowly, as frost creeps across a windowpane in the night, the world began to forget.

When the Tower’s enemies gathered in that dark, those who had kept the fire would not let the whole of it be taken. They broke the Flame into Embers, and scattered to the far and hidden hearths of the world, each bearing a shard against the ruin. One such Ember came at last to Runehold, and burns there yet, in your hearth-rune. You are its keeper now, the appointed Archon — and should that fire fail, Runehold fails with it, and one more light goes out of the world.
Yet before the first season turns, let it be told who you are: your colour and your coming, and the hard things already done to keep this small fire alight.

steps.title: Your Circle
steps.body: These few keep the Ember with you. They will counsel you and quarrel among themselves; lead them well.

steps.title: Your Colour
steps.prompt: Every Wizard leans toward one of the seven schools. Which do you name your own?

options.label: Red
options.desc: Coin, courts, and the Gilded Reach. Power bought and sold.
options.saga: You claimed the Red for your own — the coin-magic of courts.

options.label: Yellow
options.desc: The bending of time and season. Patience made into power.
options.saga: You claimed the Yellow — the slow magic of time and season.

options.label: Brown
options.desc: Earth, craft, and the practical working. Magic that feeds a hold.
options.saga: You claimed the Brown — plain, practical magic that feeds a hold.

options.label: Green
options.desc: Growing things and the wild. Nurture against the dark.
options.saga: You claimed the Green — the wild magic of growing things.

options.label: Blue
options.desc: Lore and memory; the Bastion and its Athenaeum. Knowledge above all.
options.saga: You claimed the Blue — the lore-magic of the Bastion, and set memory above all.

options.label: Purple
options.desc: Chaos and the unmaking of certainty. Power without a leash.
options.saga: You claimed the Purple — chaos-magic, power without a leash.

options.label: White
options.desc: Order and clarity against the Forgetting. The wall that holds.
options.saga: You claimed the White — the order-magic that holds against the Forgetting.

steps.title: How You Came to Lead
steps.prompt: The seat of Archon was not empty. How did it become yours?

options.label: The dying keeper named you
options.desc: The last Archon gave you the fire with their final breath. The coven honours the choice — and watches to see if it was wise.
options.saga: You came to the seat by the dying keeper's last word, and the coven honoured the choice.

options.label: The coven raised you
options.desc: They chose you from among themselves. Their faith in you is real — and yours to keep or lose.
options.saga: The coven raised you to the seat with open hands.

options.label: You took it by force
options.desc: The old Archon is a Forgotten Soul now, and not by the Flame's choosing. The coven follows — warily.
options.saga: You took the seat by force; the old Archon is a Forgotten Soul, and not by the Flame's will.

options.label: You are the last of the old Circle
options.desc: The others became Souls one by one, and the seat fell to you because no one else was left to take it. You lead by attrition, and you carry all their names.
options.saga: You came to the seat because you were the last of the old Circle left standing, and you carry all their names.

steps.title: The Winter the Ember Nearly Died
steps.prompt: Last winter the hearth-rune guttered low, and something had to be given to keep it lit. What did you give?

options.label: You burned the old archives
options.desc: You fed the coven's oldest books to the fire — knowledge traded for warmth, and the fire held.
options.saga: In the winter the Ember guttered low, you burned the old archives to keep it lit — knowledge for warmth.

options.label: You sold a Wizard's service to Red
options.desc: You pledged one of your own to the Gilded Reach for a year, and bought fuel enough with the coin.
options.saga: In the lean winter, you pledged a Wizard's year of service to the Gilded Reach to buy the fire its fuel.

options.label: You let the eldest go cold
options.desc: You gave the fire what warmth there was, and let three of the old die of the frost. You keep their names.
options.saga: In the killing frost, you let three of the eldest go cold rather than let the Ember die — and you keep their names still.

options.label: You gave a willing Wizard to the Flame
options.desc: One of your own walked into the Ember of their own will, and it flared bright and hungry on what they carried. The coven does not speak of it.
options.saga: In the dying of the light, a Wizard of your own walked willingly into the Ember, and it flared bright on what they carried.

steps.title: Steel and Rune
steps.prompt: The Warrior-Guild and Runehold are old neighbours. When steel and rune last had cause to meet, what did you do?

options.label: You stood with them
options.desc: You fought shoulder to shoulder with the Warriors against a common threat, and the hold held. Steel remembers a debt.
options.saga: The last time steel and rune met at Runehold, you stood shoulder to shoulder with the Warriors, and the hold held.

options.label: You turned them away
options.desc: You met the Warriors at the gate with cold courtesy and no bloodshed, and sent them on their road. They will not forget the slight.
options.saga: The last time the Warriors came to Runehold's gate, you turned them away with cold courtesy and no bloodshed.

options.label: You struck first
options.desc: You did not wait for the first blow. Steel has remembered Runehold ever since — and sharpens for it.
options.saga: The last time steel threatened Runehold, you struck first — and steel has remembered it since.

options.label: You have never faced them
options.desc: In your time, rune and steel have never crossed at Runehold. There is no debt between you, and no grudge — only a wary, watching peace.
options.saga: In your keeping, steel and rune have never crossed at Runehold — only a wary, watching peace.

pool.title: Your First Working
pool.prompt: Every Wizard remembers the first magic they ever worked. What was yours?

options.label: A candle that would not go out
options.desc: A small, stubborn flame you lit as a child and could not put out for three days. You have kept fires ever since.
options.saga: Your first working was a candle-flame that would not go out — a small, stubborn light.

options.label: A locked door, opened with a word
options.desc: A door barred against you swung wide at a word you didn't know you knew. Nothing has been quite closed to you since.
options.saga: Your first working was a locked door opened with a single spoken word.

options.label: A wound you closed by will
options.desc: You sealed a dying friend's wound with nothing but your will — or you didn't, and it has driven you ever since.
options.saga: Your first working was a wound closed by will alone — or one that never would close again.

options.label: A storm you could not call back
options.desc: You reached for the sky in a child's temper and it answered — too well. Someone paid for the rain you raised, and you have been careful with power since.
options.saga: Your first working was a storm you called in temper and could not call back, and someone paid for the rain.

pool.title: What Magic Is
pool.prompt: The wise argue over what magic truly is. What do you believe?

options.label: Memory
options.desc: Magic is the world remembering how it once worked — and it is being forgotten.
options.saga: You hold that magic is the world's own memory, and that it is going out.

options.label: A gift
options.desc: Magic is given, from the Flame or something older, to those who are meant to carry it.
options.saga: You hold that magic is a gift, given to those meant to carry it.

options.label: A tool
options.desc: Magic is a power like any other, to be wielded by whoever is strong enough to hold it.
options.saga: You hold that magic is a tool, for whoever is strong enough to wield it.

options.label: A debt
options.desc: Every working is borrowed, and the world keeps the ledger. The Forgetting, you suspect, is the debt coming due.
options.saga: You hold that magic is a debt borrowed against the world — and that the Forgetting is the ledger coming due.

pool.title: Your Deepest Fear
pool.prompt: Every keeper carries a dread they do not speak aloud. What is yours?

options.label: That the Flame will take you
options.desc: You have felt the Ember's hunger turn toward you in the dark. You fear the day it stops asking.
options.saga: In the dark you fear most that the Flame will one day take you, and stop asking first.

options.label: That the coven will lose faith
options.desc: A hold is held by belief before it is held by walls. You fear the morning the Circle stops believing in you.
options.saga: You fear most the morning the Circle stops believing — for a hold is held by faith before walls.

options.label: That you will be forgotten
options.desc: Not death — erasure. That your name and works will slip from the world as if you had never kept the fire at all.
options.saga: You fear not death but erasure — that your name will slip from the world as if you had never kept the fire.

options.label: That the Fracture will swallow the hold
options.desc: You have watched other hearths crack from within, cult against cult, and go dark. You fear Runehold is only slower.
options.saga: You fear the Fracture more than any foe — that Runehold will crack from within, as other hearths have, and go dark.

pool.title: A Mark You Bear
pool.prompt: The keeping of a fire leaves its sign. What mark do you carry?

options.label: A burn from the Ember
options.desc: A hand's-breadth of scar where the Flame once touched you and did not let go easily. It aches when the fire is low.
options.saga: You bear a burn from the Ember where it touched you once and did not let go — it aches when the fire runs low.

options.label: Runes that will not fade
options.desc: Sigils you worked into your own skin in a hard season, still faintly warm. They have never once cooled.
options.saga: You bear runes worked into your own skin in a hard season that have never once cooled.

options.label: An eye that sees the Forgetting
options.desc: One eye clouded pale, and through it you can see the Forgetting coming — a greyness on a thing, a day before it is lost.
options.saga: You bear one pale eye that sees the Forgetting coming — a greyness on a thing, a day before it is lost.

options.label: A name you can no longer remember
options.desc: There is a name the Forgetting has already taken from you. You suspect, in the small hours, that it was your own.
options.saga: You bear a name the Forgetting has already taken — and fear, in the small hours, that it was your own.

roles.label: Warden
roles.desc: Walks the rune-wards against the Fracture.

roles.label: Loremaster
roles.desc: Deepens the runes against the Forgetting.

roles.label: Quartermaster
roles.desc: Works the market roads for coin.

roles.label: Chaplain
roles.desc: Keeps the coven's heart through the dark.

roles.label: Conjurer
roles.desc: Tends the coven's flow of mana.

scene.title: The Ember in the Ruin
scene.intro: {party} send word from the dead hold far to the east: they have found it, the dormant Ember the rumours promised, banked low in a hearth-rune the cold spared. It is theirs to carry home — but a sleeping Ember is a quiet burden, and a woken one a bright and hunted beacon. Wake it now for the strength of the road, or bear it cold and unseen?

advisors.text: Wake it, Archon. A living Ember warms the ones who carry it and answers when they call — and it deserves to come home awake, not smuggled like stolen bread.
Cold, and hidden. A lit Ember on an open road is a torch held over one's own head. Let them carry it dark and let no one on that road know what they hold.

choices.label: Word: wake the Ember for the road

win.text: {party} coax the dormant Ember awake, and it flares small and glad against the cold. It lends them warmth and a little of its old strength — and it shines, now, for any eye on the long road east that knows to look.
win.saga: {party} woke the dormant Ember in the eastern ruin; it warmed them for the road, and shone for every eye that knew to look.

choices.label: Word: bear it cold and unseen

win.text: {party} wrap the sleeping Ember in ash-cloth and shoulder it like any other burden. It gives them nothing on the road — no warmth, no strength — but it draws no eyes either, and on this road that may be the greater gift.
win.saga: {party} bore the dormant Ember home cold and hidden, wrapped in ash-cloth and drawing no eyes on the eastern road.

scene.title: The Hunted Road
scene.intro: Word comes back tense and short: the road home is not empty. Someone knows what {party} carry — raiders, or a cult's outriders, shadowing them through the ash and closing slow. The Ember will not carry itself past them. It must be slipped past in the dark, or fought clear.

advisors.text: Slip them, Archon. Tell our people to go by night and by the bad ground, and let the hunters chase a rumour while the Ember goes home the quiet way.
Or break them. A hunter taught once that Runehold's fire bites back is a hunter who tells the others to leave our roads alone. Have them fight it clear.

choices.label: Word: slip past the hunters in the dark

win.text: {party} go to ground by day and move by the bad ground at night, and the hunters chase shadows and old campfires while the Ember slips through their fingers. By the time they understand, Runehold's borders are close.
win.saga: {party} slipped the hunters on the eastern road by night and bad ground, and carried the Ember clear while their pursuers chased shadows.

lose.text: A moon too bright, a footprint left too plain — the hunters cut the trail and close in fast. There is no more slipping now; the road ahead is teeth, and {party} must come through them or not at all.
lose.saga: {party} were run down on the eastern road while bearing the Ember home, the hunters closing in for a fight.

choices.label: Word: fight the hunters clear of the road

win.text: {party} turn on their hunters in a narrow place and light the ash with a working, and the survivors run for softer country. Runehold's fire has bitten, and word of it will run the roads ahead of them. The Ember comes on.
win.saga: {party} turned on the hunters shadowing the Ember and broke them with rune and fire, and carried it on toward home.

lose.text: The hunters were more, and readier, than {party} judged. The fight goes badly in the ash, and it is all they can do to hold onto the Ember and their lives and stagger on toward home, bloodied.
lose.saga: {party} fought the hunters for the Ember and were bloodied for it, staggering on toward home with the fire barely held.

scene.title: The Ember Comes Home
scene.intro: The watch on Runehold's wall calls it down first: {party} on the eastern road at dusk, footsore and ash-grey — and cradled between them, wrapped and whole, another Ember for the hearth. One more shard of the Sacred Flame come home out of the ruin of the world.

advisors.text: Ring the hearth-bell, Archon. Every Ember that comes home is a candle lit against the long dark — and this one our own hands carried out of the ruin.

choices.label: Bring the Ember to Runehold's hearth

win.text: {party} lay the recovered Ember beside Runehold's own, and for a moment the two fires lean together and remember. The hold is warmer, brighter, and one shard nearer whole than it was. The Circle keeps the day in the Saga.
win.saga: {party} bore a recovered Ember home to Runehold's hearth — one more shard of the Sacred Flame carried out of the ruin and set beside your own.

scene.title: The Ambush
scene.intro: The hunters have {party} cornered in a dry gully with the Ember between them, and there is no slipping and no more road. It comes to a hard, simple thing: give up the fire and buy the party's lives, or fight through and gamble everything — the Ember, and the ones who carry it — on coming out the far side.

advisors.text: Give them the fire, Archon. An Ember can be sought again. The Wizards who carry it cannot be rolled fresh from the ash. Buy them back.
Fight through. If we give up an Ember on a raider's say-so, we will give up the next on the next road, and the one after. Better to bleed for it now than be bled for it forever.

choices.label: Give up the Ember to save the party

win.text: {party} set the Ember down in the dust and step back from it, and the hunters take their prize and let them live. The party comes home whole and empty-handed, and the fire they carried so far burns now in some other, hungrier hold.
win.saga: {party} gave up the recovered Ember to raiders to save their own lives, and came home whole but empty-handed.

choices.label: Fight through and keep the Ember

win.text: {party} put their backs to the Ember and their fire in the hunters' faces, and by the end of it the gully is quiet and the road ahead is open. Bloodied, lessened, but unbroken — and the Ember is still theirs to carry home.
win.saga: {party} fought through the ambush in the dry gully and kept the Ember, bloodied but unbroken, and carried it on toward home.

lose.text: The gully was a good place to die, and the hunters knew it. {party} fall over the Ember they would not give up, and the raiders take it from their hands. Runehold will not see them, or the fire, again.
lose.saga: {party} fought to keep the Ember in the ambush and fell over it; the raiders took both the fire and their lives.

scene.title: The Road to the Lists
scene.intro: {name} sends word from the road to the tourney-town, where holds and guilds send champions to fight for a purse and a season's glory. {They} ask{s} one thing before the first bout: is this to be won clean, on craft and courage — or should {they} carry a quiet working, and make certain?

advisors.text: Clean, Archon. A crown of cheating is a crown of glass. If we win, let it be ours in truth.
Everyone at those lists is cheating a little. Tell {name} to carry the working. Winning is the reputation; how, is only gossip.

choices.label: Fight clean — craft and courage only

win.text: You send back one word: honestly. {name} sets {their} jaw and rides on to the lists with nothing up {their} sleeve but skill.
win.saga: You sent {name} to the champion's tourney to fight clean, on craft and courage alone.

choices.label: Send a quiet working to make certain

win.text: You send {name} a working folded small — a whisper to slow a blade, to steady a hand. It is not honest. It may be enough.
win.saga: You sent {name} to the tourney with a quiet working hidden — victory made certain, if none should see.

scene.title: The Early Bouts
scene.intro: {name} has come through the first rounds, and sends word by fast rider. The field narrows to the sharp and the lucky, and the crowd has begun to chant a name — {theirs}.

advisors.text: {name} is fighting beautifully, Archon. Whatever we told {them} to do, tell {them} to keep doing it.

choices.label: Word: fight on, and fairly

win.text: {name} wins {their} bracket on skill alone, and the honest crowd loves {them} for it. On to the final.
win.saga: {name} fought clean through the tourney's early bouts and won a place in the final on skill alone.

choices.label: Word: keep the working hidden and win

win.text: The working does its quiet work and no marshal is the wiser. {name} advances, {their} secret intact and {their} conscience less so. On to the final.
win.saga: {name} carried the hidden working through the tourney's early bouts unseen, and bought a place in the final.

lose.text: A grey-robed adjudicator feels the working's edge and calls it aloud. {name} is not disqualified — nothing is proven — but the whole field watches {them} now, and the crowd's love has curdled to suspicion.
lose.saga: {name}'s hidden working was half-caught at the tourney; nothing was proven, but suspicion followed {them} into the final.

scene.title: The Final Bout
scene.intro: The final. {name} faces the tourney's iron favourite before every hold and guild that sent a champion. Whatever Runehold did to get {them} here, it comes down to this ring, this bout, this breath.

advisors.text: One bout, Archon. Whatever {name} carries into that ring — skill or secret — let {them} spend all of it now.

choices.label: Let {name} fight the final

win.text: {name} takes the favourite in three passes and the purse and the crown with it. Runehold's name rings around the lists — a small hearth with a champion. {They} ride{s} home in glory.
win.saga: {name} won the champion's tourney before every hold and guild, and carried the crown and purse home to Runehold.

lose.text: {name} fights well and loses well, beaten clean by a better hand in the ring. No shame in it — but no crown either. {They} come{s} home the runner-up, and a little wiser.
lose.saga: {name} reached the final of the champion's tourney and was beaten clean — runner-up, and no shame in it.

scene.title: Into the Seam
scene.intro: {party} send word from the lip of the Fracture-seam: they have found a way down, a throat of cold light that goes deeper than any lamp can follow. The air below tastes of a season that never happened. They ask how to go — carefully, roped and slow, or fast, before the seam notices them?

advisors.text: Slow, Archon. A place that eats memory is not a place to hurry through — every handhold they forget on the way down is one they need on the way up.
Fast. The seam is asleep and the deep is far. Tell them to be down and back before the thing the colour of nothing rolls over and opens an eye.

choices.label: Word: descend slow and roped

win.text: {party} go down a handspan at a time, marking the way behind them against the forgetting. It is slow, and it is sane, and by the season's turn they are deep and still themselves.
win.saga: {party} descended into the Fracture-seam slow and roped, marking every step against the forgetting.

choices.label: Word: descend fast, before the seam wakes

win.text: {party} go down quick and reckless, and reach the deep with their nerve intact and half their marks forgotten behind them. Faster — but the way back is a rumour now.
win.saga: {party} plunged fast into the Fracture-seam, reaching the deep quickly but leaving the way home half-forgotten behind them.

scene.title: The First Fragments
scene.intro: Word comes back changed, the runner's hand shaking on the page. In the deep of the seam {party} have found fragments — shards of the world's first memory, the way things knew how to be before the Forgetting. It could be read, if a mind is strong enough to hold it. It could also unmake the mind that tries.

advisors.text: This is why we sent them, Archon. Tell them to read — carefully, and only what they can carry. A single true page of the first memory is worth a year of lamp-oil scholarship.
Or tell them to touch nothing. A mind is a small cup to pour the world's first memory into. It spills, and what spills does not come back.

choices.label: Word: read the fragments, carefully

win.text: {party} read slow, and hold. Page by page the deep gives up a little of how the world once knew itself, and they copy it down with steady hands. The coven will be wiser for this, and older about the eyes.
win.saga: In the deep of the Fracture, {party} read fragments of the world's first memory and held their minds — a page of how things once knew how to be.

lose.text: The fragments are too much, too fast. Something in the reading comes loose in {party}, and the seam around them begins, very gently, to come apart. They stop reading. They may have stopped too late.
lose.saga: {party} reached into the Fracture's first memory and could not hold it; the reading came loose in them, and the seam began to unmake.

scene.title: The Heart of the Seam
scene.intro: At the very heart of the seam, {party} find it: not a fragment but the whole of a memory, turning slow in the cold light — the world remembering, all at once, how magic was done before the Flame broke. They could take a shard of it and climb for daylight. Or they could reach for the whole, here, where it is anchored, and risk everything on one impossible grasp.

advisors.text: A shard and a safe climb, Archon. What they can carry out alive is worth infinitely more than what they die reaching for.
The whole memory, once, here, where it is anchored — we may never stand in such a place again. If they can hold it, the road back from the Forgetting opens under our feet.

choices.label: Word: take a shard and climb for daylight

win.text: {party} break off what a mind can carry and begin the long climb up, the shard of first memory burning steady and cold against the chest. They come home wiser and whole, and that is no small thing to carry out of the Fracture.
win.saga: {party} took a shard of the world's first memory from the heart of the seam and climbed home wiser and whole.

choices.label: Word: reach for the whole memory, here where it is anchored

win.text: {party} reach into the whole of it, and for one impossible breath they hold the world's first memory entire — how magic was done, how it was meant to be, all of it. They cannot keep it. But they carry out enough, and something in them has seen the shape of the road home from the long forgetting.
win.saga: {party} reached for the whole of the world's first memory in the Fracture's heart and held it, one impossible breath — and carried out a glimpse of the road back from the Forgetting.

lose.text: The whole memory gives more than any mind can hold, and it takes {party} in exchange for the reaching. The seam folds shut over them, satisfied, and the cold light goes out. They are the Fracture's now.
lose.saga: {party} reached for the whole of the world's first memory and it took them; the Fracture's heart closed over them and kept them.

scene.title: The Unmaking
scene.intro: The seam is coming apart around {party}, slow and patient, the cold light beginning to forget the shape of the walls that hold it up. They send word by a runner who can no longer quite remember his own name: cut losses and climb now, or press deeper for the memory while there is still a deeper to reach?

advisors.text: Out, Archon. Now. A hold can survive an empty-handed party. It cannot survive learning what the bottom of an unmaking seam looks like.
They are already this deep and already this changed. Tell them to press on — if there is a memory to be had at the bottom of this, it is worth what the climb will cost.

choices.label: Word: cut losses and climb out now

win.text: {party} abandon the reading and climb for the light, the seam unmaking the way behind them as fast as they can leave it. They reach the lip with nothing but their lives and the cold — but their lives are enough.
win.saga: {party} fled the unmaking Fracture-seam empty-handed, climbing out just ahead of the light forgetting the walls.

choices.label: Word: press deeper before the seam is gone

win.text: {party} go down into the unmaking rather than out of it, and against every sense they wrest a shard of the first memory from the collapsing deep before they turn and run for the light. They come home changed, and carrying something worth the change.
win.saga: {party} pressed into the unmaking Fracture-seam and tore a shard of the first memory from the collapsing deep before fleeing for the light.

lose.text: There is no shard, and there is no more up. The unmaking reaches {party} before they reach the light, and the seam closes over the place where they were as though it had forgotten they were ever there.
lose.saga: {party} pressed deeper into the unmaking seam and it closed over them, forgetting they had ever been there.

scene.title: The Wanderer Returns
scene.intro: It has been the better part of a year since {name} walked out of Runehold's gate to follow a thread of old lore into the ash. Tonight the watch calls down: a figure on the road, coming home. But the season is late, the road is long, and the Forgetting takes travellers as surely as it takes workings.

advisors.text: {name} was strong of mind, Archon. If anyone could walk that road and come back {themselves}, it is {them}. Go down and meet {them} — you will know at the gate.
Pray it is {name} who comes through that gate. The road gives back what it took, but not always as it took it.

choices.label: Go down to the gate and meet the traveller

win.text: It is {name} — thinner, wind-burned, older about the eyes, but {them}, whole and knowing you. {They} carr{ies} a satchel of lore worth the year {they} spent, and the hearth is warmer tonight for {their} return.
win.saga: {name} came home from the long road whole and {themselves}, bearing a year's worth of hard-won lore.

lose.text: The figure that stops at the edge of the firelight wears {name}'s face and {name}'s cloak — but the eyes are wrong, and it does not answer to {their} name. The road gave something back. It is not, quite, who set out.
lose.saga: What came home on the long road wore {name}'s face, but the Forgetting had been at {them} — a Soul stood where {name} had been.

scene.title: Name the Returned
scene.intro: The Soul waits at the edge of the firelight to be known. It came home wearing {name}'s face — but the Flame has rewritten what is behind it, and the coven has learned that a returning Soul is not always the one who left. Sometimes it is one of your own, long lost, come back in a borrowed shape. Name it truly, and you give it a kind of peace. Name it wrong, and the Circle will wonder what else you have forgotten.

scene.title: The Pyramid's Foot
scene.intro: Word comes back from {party}: they have reached the nameless pyramid in the ash-flats, a black tooth of stone older than the Burning. Its one door is sealed with a rune that drinks the light. They send a runner for your word — force it, or find the patient way?

advisors.text: A door is only a wall that gave up once. Tell them to break it, Archon — we did not send them to admire the masonry.
Old seals were set by someone. Tell them to look for the maker's own way in, before they wake whatever the seal was keeping.

choices.label: Send word: break the seal with power

win.text: They pour the working into the rune until it cracks, and the black door grinds inward on a breath of very old air.
win.saga: {party} broke the light-drinking seal of the nameless pyramid with sheer power, and walked into the dark beneath it.

lose.text: The rune drinks the working whole and gives nothing back. Worse — something deeper in the stone answers, and the party pulls back to regroup.
lose.saga: {party} tried to break the pyramid's seal by force; it drank their working, and something within stirred.

choices.label: Send word: find the maker's hidden way

win.text: Days of cold searching, and Nyx's runner reports a servants' stair beneath a fallen slab. They go down quietly, the seal untouched.
win.saga: {party} found the maker's own hidden stair into the nameless pyramid, and went down without waking the seal.

scene.title: The Antechamber of Names
scene.intro: The party is deep now, and their season's word is uneasy. They have come to a hall whose walls are carved floor to vault with names — thousands of them, in a script that hurts to read. Some of the names, {party} swear, are of the living. One of yours is on the wall.

advisors.text: A hall of names in a forgetting world is not decoration, Archon. It is a ledger. Tell them to copy what they can and touch nothing.
If our name is written here, this place has a claim on us. Tell them to leave an offering, and go carefully to the heart.

choices.label: Have them copy the ledger of names

win.text: They fill three books before their lamps gutter. Whatever this pyramid was, it kept a record of the world's memory — and now, in part, so do you.
win.saga: In the pyramid's antechamber {party} copied a great ledger of names — a record of the world's memory, salvaged for Runehold.

lose.text: The script writhes as they copy it, and two of the books come away blank. They press on toward the heart, unsettled and a little less certain of their own names.
lose.saga: {party} tried to copy the pyramid's ledger of names, but the script would not hold to the page.

choices.label: Press straight for the heart of the pyramid

win.text: They leave the names unread and go down toward the heart, quicker but blinder for it.
win.saga: {party} pressed past the pyramid's hall of names, leaving its ledger unread, and went down toward the heart.

scene.title: The Heart of the Pyramid
scene.intro: At the pyramid's heart, {party} find a chamber holding a single object: a rune-cut stone that hums like a struck bell and remembers, they say, everything. It could be carried home — or they could work it here, where its power is anchored, and risk waking the pyramid fully.

advisors.text: Take it and go, Archon. A bird in the hand outlives a working in a haunted vault.
Anchored power is power doubled. Tell them to use it here, once, before it is ours to keep — we may never stand in such a place again.

choices.label: Carry the singing stone home

win.text: They wrap the stone and climb for daylight. It sings all the way home, and Runehold is richer in memory and wonder for it.
win.saga: {party} bore the singing memory-stone home from the heart of the nameless pyramid — a treasure of old memory for Runehold.

choices.label: Work the stone here, where it is anchored

win.text: They draw on the anchored stone and it answers past all hope — a season's forgotten workings come flooding back into their hands. They carry both the knowing and the stone up into the light.
win.saga: At the pyramid's heart {party} worked the anchored memory-stone and drew back a flood of forgotten magic — then bore the stone home besides.

lose.text: The stone gives more than they can hold. The pyramid wakes around them, the stair folds shut, and the dark takes the party with the working half-done.
lose.saga: {party} reached too deep into the pyramid's anchored stone; it woke the tomb around them, and they did not climb back into the light.

expeditions.title: The Pilgrimage to the Rival Hearth

scene.title: The Road to the Rival Hearth
scene.intro: Word comes back from {party}: they are three weeks on the road to the hold that keeps another living Ember, and near its borders now. How they come to the door will shape how the door opens. They ask your word — as humble pilgrims, or as equals bearing a fire of their own?

advisors.text: Have them go humbly, Archon. Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole — and no one opens a door to a raised chin.
Humility is a posture, not a surrender. Tell them to bow low and watch everything — a hearth shows its weaknesses to a guest it pities.

choices.label: Word: go as humble pilgrims

win.text: {party} lay down their staves at the treeline and approach the rival hearth with open hands and bowed heads. The watchers on the wall soften, a little.
win.saga: You sent {party} to the rival hearth as humble pilgrims, hands open and heads bowed.

choices.label: Word: go as equals, bearing a fire of your own

win.text: {party} approach the rival hearth as one Circle to another, Runehold's name spoken plainly. The watchers stiffen, but they do not reach for wards.
win.saga: You sent {party} to the rival hearth as equals, Runehold's name spoken plainly at their gate.

scene.title: The Threshold
scene.intro: The rival Archon meets {party} on the stone threshold, a working half-shaped in her hand. "We know what you carry," she says. "We know what you want. Two Embers are stronger than one — but not if one hold swallows the other." {party} send for your word: sue for an accord, or take the fire while they stand so close to it?

advisors.text: Treat with them, Archon. This is how the Flame comes home — thread by thread, not by conquest. Have our people sue for an accord.
Or take theirs. One great fire in one strong hold outweighs two frightened ones. They would do the same to us, given the nerve — and our people are already inside the door.

choices.label: Word: sue for an accord between the hearths

win.text: {party} speak long and carefully, and something in the rival Archon's eyes eases. She sets her half-working down. There is a door here after all.
win.saga: At the rival hearth's threshold, {party} spoke well, and the rival Archon set aside her working to hear an accord.

lose.text: The words come out a shade too eager, and the rival Archon hears the want beneath them. Her hand does not leave the working. The door, for now, is closed.
lose.saga: {party} sued for an accord at the rival hearth, but the want showed through their words, and the door stayed shut.

choices.label: Word: take their Ember while you stand at the fire

win.text: You send the cold word back down the road: take it. {party} exchange a look on the threshold, and reach for the working that will open the way to another hold's fire.
win.saga: You sent {party} to seize the rival hearth's Ember by force, standing already at its very fire.

scene.title: The Accord Sworn
scene.intro: A season of careful talk, and it is done: {party} return to the threshold to seal it. Two hearths, two Embers, sworn to keep faith and share what they remember. Not a gathering yet — but the first true thread of one, and it holds.

advisors.text: This is the road home, Archon. Not the whole of it — but the first stone of it, and we laid it without a single working drawn in anger.

choices.label: Seal the accord and bring the party home

win.text: {party} clasp hands over both hearth-runes, and for a moment the two Embers lean toward each other like they remember being one Flame. They come home with an accord and an ally where there was a stranger.
win.saga: {party} sealed an accord between Runehold and the rival hearth — two Embers sworn to keep faith, the first thread of a gathering.

scene.title: The Cold Door
scene.intro: There is no more to be done here. The rival hearth keeps its door shut and its fire close, and {party} have worn out what welcome they had. They send for your word, but there is only one word to send: come home.

advisors.text: We have not made an enemy, Archon — only failed to make a friend. Bring them home before failing becomes the other thing.

choices.label: Call the party home from the cold door

win.text: {party} bow to the shut door one last time and take the long road home, empty-handed but whole. The rival hearth watches them go, and does not follow.
win.saga: Rebuffed at the rival hearth's cold door, {party} came home empty-handed but whole, no enemy made.

scene.title: The Taking of the Fire
scene.intro: It is done in a single terrible working, or it is not done at all. {party} stand inside a rival hold's ward-lines, reaching for a fire that is not theirs, and the rival Circle is already turning toward them with murder in their hands.

advisors.text: No half-measures now, Archon. They must take it clean and fast, or the rival Circle will make Souls of them on their own threshold.

choices.label: Take the Ember by force of magic

win.text: It is quick, and it is ugly. {party} tear the Ember from its hearth and are gone before the rival Circle can close — Runehold has two fires now, and the certain knowledge that every other hearth will bar its door and sharpen something for it.
win.saga: {party} tore a second Ember from the rival hearth by force — Runehold burns with two fires now, and every other hearth has turned against it.

lose.text: The rival Circle was readier than yours. The working shatters, the ward-lines close, and {party} do not come back out of that hold — not as themselves, not at all.
lose.saga: {party} reached for the rival hearth's Ember by force and were overmatched; they did not come home from that hold.
